17/*                       _                            _ _
18 *   _ __ ___   ___   __| |    _ __ _____      ___ __(_) |_ ___
19 *  | '_ ` _ \ / _ \ / _` |   | '__/ _ \ \ /\ / / '__| | __/ _ \
20 *  | | | | | | (_) | (_| |   | | |  __/\ V  V /| |  | | ||  __/
21 *  |_| |_| |_|\___/ \__,_|___|_|  \___| \_/\_/ |_|  |_|\__\___|
22 *                       |_____|
23 *
24 *  URL Rewriting Module
25 *
26 *  This module uses a rule-based rewriting engine (based on a
27 *  regular-expression parser) to rewrite requested URLs on the fly.
28 *
29 *  It supports an unlimited number of additional rule conditions (which can
30 *  operate on a lot of variables, even on HTTP headers) for granular
31 *  matching and even external database lookups (either via plain text
32 *  tables, DBM hash files or even external processes) for advanced URL
33 *  substitution.
34 *
35 *  It operates on the full URLs (including the PATH_INFO part) both in
36 *  per-server context (httpd.conf) and per-dir context (.htaccess) and even
37 *  can generate QUERY_STRING parts on result.   The rewriting result finally
38 *  can lead to internal subprocessing, external request redirection or even
39 *  to internal proxy throughput.
40 *
41 *  This module was originally written in April 1996 and
42 *  gifted exclusively to the The Apache Software Foundation in July 1997 by
43 *
44 *      Ralf S. Engelschall
45 *      rse engelschall.com
46 *      www.engelschall.com
47 */ view japanese
48

111static ap_dbd_t *(*dbd_acquire)(request_rec*) = NULL;
112static void (*dbd_prepare)(server_rec*, const char*, const char*) = NULL;
113static const char* really_last_key = "rewrite_really_last";
114
115/*
116 * in order to improve performance on running production systems, you
117 * may strip all rewritelog code entirely from mod_rewrite by using the
118 * -DREWRITELOG_DISABLED compiler option.
119 *
120 * DO NOT USE THIS OPTION FOR PUBLIC BINARY RELEASES. Otherwise YOU are
121 * responsible for answering all the mod_rewrite questions out there.
122 */ view japanese
123/* If logging is limited to APLOG_DEBUG or lower, disable rewrite log, too */ view japanese
124#ifdef  APLOG_MAX_LOGLEVEL
125#if     APLOG_MAX_LOGLEVEL < APLOG_TRACE1
126#ifndef REWRITELOG_DISABLED
127#define REWRITELOG_DISABLED
128#endif
129#endif
130#endif
131
132#ifndef REWRITELOG_DISABLED
133#ifdef AP_HAVE_C99
134#define rewritelog(...) do_rewritelog(__LINE__, __VA_ARGS__)
135#else
136#define rewritelog do_rewritelog
137#endif
138#define REWRITELOG_MODE  ( APR_UREAD | APR_UWRITE | APR_GREAD | APR_WREAD )
139#define REWRITELOG_FLAGS ( APR_WRITE | APR_APPEND | APR_CREATE )
140
141#else /* !REWRITELOG_DISABLED */
142
143#ifdef AP_HAVE_C99
144#define rewritelog(...)
145#else
146#define rewritelog do_rewritelog
147#endif
148#endif /* REWRITELOG_DISABLED */ view japanese
149
150/* remembered mime-type for [T=...] */ view japanese
151#define REWRITE_FORCED_MIMETYPE_NOTEVAR "rewrite-forced-mimetype"
152#define REWRITE_FORCED_HANDLER_NOTEVAR  "rewrite-forced-handler"
153
154#define ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL "SCRIPT_URL"
155#define REDIRECT_ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL "REDIRECT_" ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL
156#define ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URI "SCRIPT_URI"

158#define CONDFLAG_NONE               (1<<0)
159#define CONDFLAG_NOCASE             (1<<1)
160#define CONDFLAG_NOTMATCH           (1<<2)
161#define CONDFLAG_ORNEXT             (1<<3)
162#define CONDFLAG_NOVARY             (1<<4)
163
164#define RULEFLAG_NONE               (1<<0)
165#define RULEFLAG_FORCEREDIRECT      (1<<1)
166#define RULEFLAG_LASTRULE           (1<<2)
167#define RULEFLAG_NEWROUND           (1<<3)
168#define RULEFLAG_CHAIN              (1<<4)
169#define RULEFLAG_IGNOREONSUBREQ     (1<<5)
170#define RULEFLAG_NOTMATCH           (1<<6)
171#define RULEFLAG_PROXY              (1<<7)
172#define RULEFLAG_PASSTHROUGH        (1<<8)
173#define RULEFLAG_QSAPPEND           (1<<9)
174#define RULEFLAG_NOCASE             (1<<10)
175#define RULEFLAG_NOESCAPE           (1<<11)
176#define RULEFLAG_NOSUB              (1<<12)
177#define RULEFLAG_STATUS             (1<<13)
178#define RULEFLAG_ESCAPEBACKREF      (1<<14)
179#define RULEFLAG_DISCARDPATHINFO    (1<<15)
180#define RULEFLAG_QSDISCARD          (1<<16)
181#define RULEFLAG_END                (1<<17)
182#define RULEFLAG_ESCAPENOPLUS       (1<<18)
183#define RULEFLAG_QSLAST             (1<<19)
184#define RULEFLAG_QSNONE             (1<<20) /* programattic only */ view japanese
185#define RULEFLAG_ESCAPECTLS         (1<<21)
186#define RULEFLAG_UNSAFE_PREFIX_STAT (1<<22)
187#define RULEFLAG_UNSAFE_ALLOW3F     (1<<23)
188#define RULEFLAG_UNC                (1<<24)
189
190/* return code of the rewrite rule
191 * the result may be escaped - or not
192 */ view japanese
193#define ACTION_NORMAL               (1<<0)
194#define ACTION_NOESCAPE             (1<<1)
195#define ACTION_STATUS               (1<<2)
196#define ACTION_STATUS_SET           (1<<3)
197
198#define MAPTYPE_TXT                 (1<<0)
199#define MAPTYPE_DBM                 (1<<1)
200#define MAPTYPE_PRG                 (1<<2)
201#define MAPTYPE_INT                 (1<<3)
202#define MAPTYPE_RND                 (1<<4)
203#define MAPTYPE_DBD                 (1<<5)
204#define MAPTYPE_DBD_CACHE           (1<<6)
205
206#define ENGINE_DISABLED             (1<<0)
207#define ENGINE_ENABLED              (1<<1)
208
209#define OPTION_NONE                 (1<<0)
210#define OPTION_INHERIT              (1<<1)
211#define OPTION_INHERIT_BEFORE       (1<<2)
212#define OPTION_NOSLASH              (1<<3)
213#define OPTION_ANYURI               (1<<4)
214#define OPTION_MERGEBASE            (1<<5)
215#define OPTION_INHERIT_DOWN         (1<<6)
216#define OPTION_INHERIT_DOWN_BEFORE  (1<<7)
217#define OPTION_IGNORE_INHERIT       (1<<8)
218#define OPTION_IGNORE_CONTEXT_INFO  (1<<9)
219#define OPTION_LEGACY_PREFIX_DOCROOT (1<<10)
220#define OPTION_UNSAFE_PREFIX_STAT   (1<<12)

240/* for better readbility */ view japanese
241#define LEFT_CURLY  '{'
242#define RIGHT_CURLY '}'
243
244/*
245 * expansion result items on the stack to save some cycles
246 *
247 * (5 == about 2 variables like "foo%{var}bar%{var}baz")
248 */ view japanese
249#define SMALL_EXPANSION 5
250
251/*
252 * check that a subrequest won't cause infinite recursion
253 *
254 * either not in a subrequest, or in a subrequest
255 * and URIs aren't NULL and sub/main URIs differ
256 */ view japanese
257#define subreq_ok(r) (!r->main || \
258    (r->main->uri && r->uri && strcmp(r->main->uri, r->uri)))
259
260#ifndef REWRITE_MAX_ROUNDS
261#define REWRITE_MAX_ROUNDS 32000
262#endif

264/*
265 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
266 * |                                                       |
267 * |                 Types and Structures
268 * |                                                       |
269 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
270 */ view japanese
271
272typedef struct {
273    const char *datafile;          /* filename for map data files         */ view japanese
274    const char *dbmtype;           /* dbm type for dbm map data files     */ view japanese
275    const char *checkfile;         /* filename to check for map existence */ view japanese
276    const char *cachename;         /* for cached maps (txt/rnd/dbm)       */ view japanese
277    int   type;                    /* the type of the map                 */ view japanese
278    apr_file_t *fpin;              /* in  file pointer for program maps   */ view japanese
279    apr_file_t *fpout;             /* out file pointer for program maps   */ view japanese
280    apr_file_t *fperr;             /* err file pointer for program maps   */ view japanese
281    char *(*func)(request_rec *,   /* function pointer for internal maps  */ view japanese
282                  char *);
283    char **argv;                   /* argv of the external rewrite map    */ view japanese
284    const char *dbdq;              /* SQL SELECT statement for rewritemap */ view japanese
285    const char *checkfile2;        /* filename to check for map existence
286                                      NULL if only one file               */ view japanese
287    const char *user;              /* run RewriteMap program as this user */ view japanese
288    const char *group;             /* run RewriteMap program as this group */ view japanese
289} rewritemap_entry;
290
291/* special pattern types for RewriteCond */ view japanese
292typedef enum {
293    CONDPAT_REGEX = 0,
294    CONDPAT_FILE_EXISTS,
295    CONDPAT_FILE_SIZE,
296    CONDPAT_FILE_LINK,
297    CONDPAT_FILE_DIR,
298    CONDPAT_FILE_XBIT,
299    CONDPAT_LU_URL,
300    CONDPAT_LU_FILE,
301    CONDPAT_STR_LT,
302    CONDPAT_STR_LE,
303    CONDPAT_STR_EQ,
304    CONDPAT_STR_GT,
305    CONDPAT_STR_GE,
306    CONDPAT_INT_LT,
307    CONDPAT_INT_LE,
308    CONDPAT_INT_EQ,
309    CONDPAT_INT_GT,
310    CONDPAT_INT_GE,
311    CONDPAT_AP_EXPR
312} pattern_type;
313
314typedef enum {
315  RULE_RC_NOMATCH = 0,      /* the rule didn't match                        */ view japanese
316  RULE_RC_MATCH = 1,        /* a matching rule w/ substitution              */ view japanese
317  RULE_RC_NOSUB = 2,        /* a matching rule w/ no substitution           */ view japanese
318  RULE_RC_STATUS_SET = 3    /* a matching rule that has set an HTTP error
319                               to be returned in r->status */ view japanese
320} rule_return_type;
321
322typedef enum {
323  COND_RC_NOMATCH = 0,      /* the cond didn't match                        */ view japanese
324  COND_RC_MATCH = 1,        /* the cond matched                             */ view japanese
325  COND_RC_STATUS_SET = 3    /* The condition eval set a final r->status     */ view japanese
326} cond_return_type;
327
328typedef struct {
329    char           *input;   /* Input string of RewriteCond   */ view japanese
330    char           *pattern; /* the RegExp pattern string     */ view japanese
331    ap_regex_t     *regexp;  /* the precompiled regexp        */ view japanese
332    ap_expr_info_t *expr;    /* the compiled ap_expr          */ view japanese
333    int             flags;   /* Flags which control the match */ view japanese
334    pattern_type    ptype;   /* pattern type                  */ view japanese
335    int             pskip;   /* back-index to display pattern */ view japanese
336} rewritecond_entry;
337
338/* single linked list for env vars and cookies */ view japanese
339typedef struct data_item {
340    struct data_item *next;
341    char *data;
342} data_item;
343
344typedef struct {
345    apr_array_header_t *rewriteconds;/* the corresponding RewriteCond entries */ view japanese
346    char      *pattern;              /* the RegExp pattern string             */ view japanese
347    ap_regex_t *regexp;              /* the RegExp pattern compilation        */ view japanese
348    char      *output;               /* the Substitution string               */ view japanese
349    int        flags;                /* Flags which control the substitution  */ view japanese
350    char      *forced_mimetype;      /* forced MIME type of substitution      */ view japanese
351    char      *forced_handler;       /* forced content handler of subst.      */ view japanese
352    int        forced_responsecode;  /* forced HTTP response status           */ view japanese
353    data_item *env;                  /* added environment variables           */ view japanese
354    data_item *cookie;               /* added cookies                         */ view japanese
355    int        skip;                 /* number of next rules to skip          */ view japanese
356    int        maxrounds;            /* limit on number of loops with N flag  */ view japanese
357    const char *escapes;             /* specific backref escapes              */ view japanese
358    const char *noescapes;           /* specific backref chars not to escape  */ view japanese
359} rewriterule_entry;
360
361typedef struct {
362    int           state;              /* the RewriteEngine state            */ view japanese
363    int           options;            /* the RewriteOption state            */ view japanese
364    apr_hash_t         *rewritemaps;  /* the RewriteMap entries             */ view japanese
365    apr_array_header_t *rewriteconds; /* the RewriteCond entries (temp.)    */ view japanese
366    apr_array_header_t *rewriterules; /* the RewriteRule entries            */ view japanese
367    server_rec   *server;             /* the corresponding server indicator */ view japanese
368    unsigned int state_set:1;
369    unsigned int options_set:1;
370} rewrite_server_conf;
371
372typedef struct {
373    int           state;              /* the RewriteEngine state           */ view japanese
374    int           options;            /* the RewriteOption state           */ view japanese
375    apr_array_header_t *rewriteconds; /* the RewriteCond entries (temp.)   */ view japanese
376    apr_array_header_t *rewriterules; /* the RewriteRule entries           */ view japanese
377    char         *directory;          /* the directory where it applies    */ view japanese
378    const char   *baseurl;            /* the base-URL  where it applies    */ view japanese
379    unsigned int state_set:1;
380    unsigned int options_set:1;
381    unsigned int baseurl_set:1;
382} rewrite_perdir_conf;
383
384/* the (per-child) cache structures.
385 */ view japanese
386typedef struct cache {
387    apr_pool_t         *pool;
388    apr_hash_t         *maps;
389#if APR_HAS_THREADS
390    apr_thread_mutex_t *lock;
391#endif
392} cache;
393
394/* cached maps contain an mtime for the whole map and live in a subpool
395 * of the cachep->pool. That makes it easy to forget them if necessary.
396 */ view japanese
397typedef struct {
398    apr_time_t mtime;
399    apr_pool_t *pool;
400    apr_hash_t *entries;
401} cachedmap;
402
403/* the regex structure for the
404 * substitution of backreferences
405 */ view japanese
406typedef struct backrefinfo {
407    const char *source;
408    ap_regmatch_t regmatch[AP_MAX_REG_MATCH];
409} backrefinfo;
410
411/* single linked list used for
412 * variable expansion
413 */ view japanese
414typedef struct result_list {
415    struct result_list *next;
416    apr_size_t len;
417    const char *string;
418} result_list;
419
420/* context structure for variable lookup and expansion
421 */ view japanese
422typedef struct {
423    request_rec *r;
424    const char  *uri;
425    const char  *vary_this;
426    const char  *vary;
427    char        *perdir;
428    backrefinfo briRR;
429    backrefinfo briRC;
430} rewrite_ctx;
431
432/*
433 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
434 * |                                                       |
435 * |                 static module data
436 * |                                                       |
437 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
438 */ view japanese
439
440/* the global module structure */ view japanese
441module AP_MODULE_DECLARE_DATA rewrite_module;
442
443/* rewritemap int: handler function registry */ view japanese
444static apr_hash_t *mapfunc_hash;
445
446/* the cache */ view japanese
447static cache *cachep;
448
449/* whether proxy module is available or not */ view japanese
450static int proxy_available;
451
452/* Locks/Mutexes */ view japanese
453static int rewrite_lock_needed = 0;
454static apr_global_mutex_t *rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire = NULL;
455static const char *rewritemap_mutex_type = "rewrite-map";
456
457/* Optional functions imported from mod_ssl when loaded: */ view japanese
458static char *escape_backref(apr_pool_t *p, const char *path,
459                            const char *escapeme, const char *noescapeme,
460                            int flags);
461
462/*
463 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
464 * |                                                       |
465 * |              rewriting logfile support
466 * |                                                       |
467 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
468 */ view japanese
469
470/* Slightly complicated definitions follow here to support
471 * compile-time choice of enabled/disabled of rewritelog both with or
472 * without C99 support, where varargs macros are only available with
473 * C99.
474 * 
475 * do_rewritelog is defined for (rewritelog enabled) or (rewritelog
476 * disabled AND no C99 support) but is an noop for the latter case and
477 * should get optimized away.
478 *
479 * For the (rewritelog disabled) case the function is defined
480 * differently for C99/non-C99. For C99, the rewritelog() macro passes
481 * __LINE__, allowing accurate logging of line numbers. For non-C99
482 * the line number used for rewritelog() tracing is always
483 * constant. */ view japanese
484
485#if !defined(REWRITELOG_DISABLED) || !defined(AP_HAVE_C99)
486
487#ifdef AP_HAVE_C99
488static void do_rewritelog(int line,
489                          request_rec *r, int level, char *perdir,
490                          const char *fmt, ...)
491        __attribute__((format(printf,5,6)));
492#else
493static void do_rewritelog(request_rec *r, int level, char *perdir,
494                          const char *fmt, ...)
495        __attribute__((format(printf,4,5)));
496#endif
497
498static void do_rewritelog(
499#ifdef AP_HAVE_C99
500                          int line,
501#endif
502                          request_rec *r, int level, char *perdir,
503                          const char *fmt, ...)
504{
505#ifndef REWRITELOG_DISABLED
506    char *logline, *text;
507    const char *rhost, *rname;
508    int redir;
509    request_rec *req;
510    va_list ap;
511
512    if (!APLOG_R_IS_LEVEL(r, APLOG_DEBUG + level))
513        return;
514
515    rhost = ap_get_useragent_host(r, REMOTE_NOLOOKUP, NULL);
516    rname = ap_get_remote_logname(r);
517
518    for (redir=0, req=r; req->prev; req = req->prev) {
519        ++redir;
520    }
521
522    va_start(ap, fmt);
523    text = apr_pvsprintf(r->pool, fmt, ap);
524    va_end(ap);
525
526    logline = apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%s %s %s [%s/sid#%pp][rid#%pp/%s%s%s] "
527                                    "%s%s%s%s",
528                           rhost ? rhost : "UNKNOWN-HOST",
529                           rname ? rname : "-",
530                           r->user ? (*r->user ? r->user : "\"\"") : "-",
531                           ap_get_server_name(r),
532                           (void *)(r->server),
533                           (void *)r,
534                           r->main ? "subreq" : "initial",
535                           redir ? "/redir#" : "",
536                           redir ? apr_itoa(r->pool, redir) : "",
537                           perdir ? "[perdir " : "",
538                           perdir ? perdir : "",
539                           perdir ? "] ": "",
540                           text);
541
542    AP_REWRITE_LOG((uintptr_t)r, level, r->main ? 0 : 1, (char *)ap_get_server_name(r), logline);
543
544    /* Intentional no APLOGNO */ view japanese
545#ifdef AP_HAVE_C99
546    ap_log_rerror(__FILE__, line, APLOG_MODULE_INDEX, APLOG_DEBUG + level, 0, r, "%s", logline);
547#else
548    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG + level, 0, r, "%s", logline);
549#endif
550    
551#endif /* !REWRITELOG_DISABLED */ view japanese
552}
553#endif /* !defined(REWRITELOG_DISABLED) || !defined(AP_HAVE_C99) */ view japanese
554
555
556/*
557 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
558 * |                                                       |
559 * |                URI and path functions
560 * |                                                       |
561 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
562 */ view japanese
563
564/* return number of chars of the scheme (incl. '://')
565 * if the URI is absolute (includes a scheme etc.)
566 * otherwise 0.
567 * If supportqs is not NULL, we return a whether or not
568 * the scheme supports a query string or not.
569 *
570 * NOTE: If you add new schemes here, please have a
571 *       look at escape_absolute_uri and splitout_queryargs.
572 *       Not every scheme takes query strings and some schemes
573 *       may be handled in a special way.
574 *
575 * XXX: we may consider a scheme registry, perhaps with
576 *      appropriate escape callbacks to allow other modules
577 *      to extend mod_rewrite at runtime.
578 */ view japanese
579static unsigned is_absolute_uri(char *uri, int *supportsqs)
580{
581    int dummy, *sqs;
582
583    sqs = (supportsqs ? supportsqs : &dummy);
584    *sqs = 0;
585    /* fast exit */
586    if (*uri == '/' || strlen(uri) <= 5) {
587        return 0;
588    }
589
590    switch (*uri++) {
591    case 'a':
592    case 'A':
593        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "jp://", 5)) {        /* ajp://    */ view japanese
594          *sqs = 1;
595          return 6;
596        }
597        break;
598
599    case 'b':
600    case 'B':
601        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "alancer://", 10)) {   /* balancer:// */ view japanese
602          *sqs = 1;
603          return 11;
604        }
605        break;
606
607    case 'f':
608    case 'F':
609        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "tp://", 5)) {        /* ftp://    */ view japanese
610            return 6;
611        }
612        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "cgi://", 6)) {       /* fcgi://   */ view japanese
613            *sqs = 1;
614            return 7;
615        }
616        break;
617
618    case 'g':
619    case 'G':
620        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "opher://", 8)) {     /* gopher:// */ view japanese
621            return 9;
622        }
623        break;
624
625    case 'h':
626    case 'H':
627        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "ttp://", 6)) {       /* http://   */ view japanese
628            *sqs = 1;
629            return 7;
630        }
631        else if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "ttps://", 7)) { /* https://  */ view japanese
632            *sqs = 1;
633            return 8;
634        }
635        else if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "2://", 4)) {    /* h2://     */ view japanese
636            *sqs = 1;
637            return 5;
638        }
639        else if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "2c://", 5)) {   /* h2c://    */ view japanese
640            *sqs = 1;
641            return 6;
642        }
643        break;
644
645    case 'l':
646    case 'L':
647        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "dap://", 6)) {       /* ldap://   */ view japanese
648            return 7;
649        }
650        break;
651
652    case 'm':
653    case 'M':
654        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "ailto:", 6)) {       /* mailto:   */ view japanese
655            *sqs = 1;
656            return 7;
657        }
658        break;
659
660    case 'n':
661    case 'N':
662        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "ews:", 4)) {         /* news:     */ view japanese
663            return 5;
664        }
665        else if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "ntp://", 6)) {  /* nntp://   */ view japanese
666            return 7;
667        }
668        break;
669
670    case 's':
671    case 'S':
672        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "cgi://", 6)) {       /* scgi://   */ view japanese
673            *sqs = 1;
674            return 7;
675        }
676        break;
677
678    case 'w':
679    case 'W':
680        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "s://", 4)) {        /* ws://     */ view japanese
681            *sqs = 1;
682            return 5;
683        }
684        else if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "ss://", 5)) {  /* wss://    */ view japanese
685            *sqs = 1;
686            return 6;
687        }
688        break;
689
690    case 'u':
691    case 'U':
692        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "nix:", 4)) {        /* unix:     */ view japanese
693            *sqs = 1;
694            return (uri[4] == '/' && uri[5] == '/') ? 7 : 5;
695        }
696    }
697
698    return 0;
699}
700
701static int is_absolute_path(const char *path)
702{
703#ifndef CASE_BLIND_FILESYSTEM
704    return (path[0] == '/');
705#else
706    return ((AP_IS_SLASH(path[0]) && path[1] == path[0])
707            || (apr_isalpha(path[0]) && path[1] == ':' && AP_IS_SLASH(path[2])));
708#endif
709}
710
711static const char c2x_table[] = "0123456789abcdef";
712
713static APR_INLINE unsigned char *c2x(unsigned what, unsigned char prefix,
714                                     unsigned char *where)
715{
716#if APR_CHARSET_EBCDIC
717    what = apr_xlate_conv_byte(ap_hdrs_to_ascii, (unsigned char)what);
718#endif /*APR_CHARSET_EBCDIC*/ view japanese
719    *where++ = prefix;
720    *where++ = c2x_table[what >> 4];
721    *where++ = c2x_table[what & 0xf];
722    return where;
723}
724
725/*
726 * Escapes a backreference in a similar way as php's urlencode does.
727 * Based on ap_os_escape_path in server/util.c
728 */ view japanese
729static char *escape_backref(apr_pool_t *p, const char *path,
730                            const char *escapeme, const char *noescapeme,
731                            int flags)
732{
733    char *copy = apr_palloc(p, 3 * strlen(path) + 1);
734    const unsigned char *s = (const unsigned char *)path;
735    unsigned char *d = (unsigned char *)copy;
736    int noplus = (flags & RULEFLAG_ESCAPENOPLUS) != 0;
737    int ctls = (flags & RULEFLAG_ESCAPECTLS) != 0;
738    unsigned char c;
739
740    while ((c = *s)) {
741        if (((ctls ? !TEST_CHAR(c, T_VCHAR_OBSTEXT) : !escapeme)
742             || (escapeme && ap_strchr_c(escapeme, c)))
743            && (!noescapeme || !ap_strchr_c(noescapeme, c))) {
744            if (apr_isalnum(c) || c == '_') {
745                *d++ = c;
746            }
747            else if (c == ' ' && !noplus) {
748                *d++ = '+';
749            }
750            else {
751                d = c2x(c, '%', d);
752            }
753        }
754        else {
755            *d++ = c;
756        }
757        ++s;
758    }
759    *d = '\0';
760    return copy;
761}
762
763/*
764 * escape absolute uri, which may or may not be path oriented.
765 * So let's handle them differently.
766 */ view japanese
767static char *escape_absolute_uri(apr_pool_t *p, char *uri, unsigned scheme)
768{
769    char *cp;
770
771    /* be safe.
772     * NULL should indicate elsewhere, that something's wrong
773     */ view japanese
774    if (!scheme || strlen(uri) < scheme) {
775        return NULL;
776    }
777
778    cp = uri + scheme;
779
780    /* scheme with authority part? */ view japanese
781    if (cp[-1] == '/') {
782        /* skip host part */ view japanese
783        while (*cp && *cp != '/') {
784            ++cp;
785        }
786
787        /* nothing after the hostpart. ready! */ view japanese
788        if (!*cp || !*++cp) {
789            return apr_pstrdup(p, uri);
790        }
791
792        /* remember the hostname stuff */ view japanese
793        scheme = cp - uri;
794
795        /* special thing for ldap.
796         * The parts are separated by question marks. From RFC 2255:
797         *     ldapurl = scheme "://" [hostport] ["/"
798         *               [dn ["?" [attributes] ["?" [scope]
799         *               ["?" [filter] ["?" extensions]]]]]]
800         */ view japanese
801        if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(uri, "ldap", 4)) {
802            char *token[5];
803            int c = 0;
804
805            token[0] = cp = apr_pstrdup(p, cp);
806            while (*cp && c < 4) {
807                if (*cp == '?') {
808                    token[++c] = cp + 1;
809                    *cp = '\0';
810                }
811                ++cp;
812            }
813
814            return apr_pstrcat(p, apr_pstrndup(p, uri, scheme),
815                                          ap_escape_uri(p, token[0]),
816                               (c >= 1) ? "?" : NULL,
817                               (c >= 1) ? ap_escape_uri(p, token[1]) : NULL,
818                               (c >= 2) ? "?" : NULL,
819                               (c >= 2) ? ap_escape_uri(p, token[2]) : NULL,
820                               (c >= 3) ? "?" : NULL,
821                               (c >= 3) ? ap_escape_uri(p, token[3]) : NULL,
822                               (c >= 4) ? "?" : NULL,
823                               (c >= 4) ? ap_escape_uri(p, token[4]) : NULL,
824                               NULL);
825        }
826    }
827
828    /* Nothing special here. Apply normal escaping. */ view japanese
829    return apr_pstrcat(p, apr_pstrndup(p, uri, scheme),
830                       ap_escape_uri(p, cp), NULL);
831}
832
833/*
834 * split out a QUERY_STRING part from
835 * the current URI string
836 */ view japanese
837static void splitout_queryargs(request_rec *r, int flags)
838{
839    char *q;
840    int split, skip;
841    int qsappend = flags & RULEFLAG_QSAPPEND;
842    int qsdiscard = flags & RULEFLAG_QSDISCARD;
843    int qslast = flags & RULEFLAG_QSLAST;
844
845    if (flags & RULEFLAG_QSNONE) {
846        rewritelog(r, 2, NULL, "discarding query string, no parse from substitution");
847        r->args = NULL;
848        return;
849    }
850
851    /* don't touch, unless it's a scheme for which a query string makes sense.
852     * See RFC 1738 and RFC 2368.
853     */ view japanese
854    if ((skip = is_absolute_uri(r->filename, &split))
855        && !split) {
856        r->args = NULL; /* forget the query that's still flying around */ view japanese
857        return;
858    }
859
860    if (qsdiscard) {
861        r->args = NULL; /* Discard query string */ view japanese
862        rewritelog(r, 2, NULL, "discarding query string");
863    }
864
865    q = qslast ? ap_strrchr(r->filename + skip, '?') : ap_strchr(r->filename + skip, '?');
866
867    if (q != NULL) {
868        char *olduri;
869        apr_size_t len;
870
871        olduri = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->filename);
872        *q++ = '\0';
873        if (qsappend) {
874            if (*q) {
875                r->args = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, q, "&" , r->args, NULL);
876            }
877        }
878        else {
879            r->args = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, q);
880        }
881
882        if (r->args) {
883           len = strlen(r->args);
884
885           if (!len) {
886               r->args = NULL;
887           }
888           else if (r->args[len-1] == '&') {
889               r->args[len-1] = '\0';
890           }
891        }
892
893        rewritelog(r, 3, NULL, "split uri=%s -> uri=%s, args=%s", olduri,
894                   r->filename, r->args ? r->args : "<none>");
895    }
896}
897
898/*
899 * strip 'http[s]://ourhost/' from URI
900 */ view japanese
901static void reduce_uri(request_rec *r)
902{
903    char *cp;
904    apr_size_t l;
905
906    cp = (char *)ap_http_scheme(r);
907    l  = strlen(cp);
908    if (   strlen(r->filename) > l+3
909        && ap_cstr_casecmpn(r->filename, cp, l) == 0
910        && r->filename[l]   == ':'
911        && r->filename[l+1] == '/'
912        && r->filename[l+2] == '/' ) {
913
914        unsigned short port;
915        char *portp, *host, *url, *scratch;
916
917        scratch = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->filename); /* our scratchpad */ view japanese
918
919        /* cut the hostname and port out of the URI */ view japanese
920        cp = host = scratch + l + 3;    /* 3 == strlen("://") */ view japanese
921        while (*cp && *cp != '/' && *cp != ':') {
922            ++cp;
923        }
924
925        if (*cp == ':') {      /* additional port given */ view japanese
926            *cp++ = '\0';
927            portp = cp;
928            while (*cp && *cp != '/') {
929                ++cp;
930            }
931            *cp = '\0';
932
933            port = atoi(portp);
934            url = r->filename + (cp - scratch);
935            if (!*url) {
936                url = "/";
937            }
938        }
939        else if (*cp == '/') { /* default port */ view japanese
940            *cp = '\0';
941
942            port = ap_default_port(r);
943            url = r->filename + (cp - scratch);
944        }
945        else {
946            port = ap_default_port(r);
947            url = "/";
948        }
949
950        /* now check whether we could reduce it to a local path... */ view japanese
951        if (ap_matches_request_vhost(r, host, port)) {
952            rewrite_server_conf *conf = 
953                ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config, &rewrite_module);
954            rewritelog(r, 3, NULL, "reduce %s -> %s", r->filename, url);
955            r->filename = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, url);
956
957            /* remember that the uri was reduced */ view japanese
958            if(!(conf->options & OPTION_LEGACY_PREFIX_DOCROOT)) {
959                apr_table_setn(r->notes, "mod_rewrite_uri_reduced", "true");
960            }
961        }
962    }
963
964    return;
965}
966
967/*
968 * add 'http[s]://ourhost[:ourport]/' to URI
969 * if URI is still not fully qualified
970 */ view japanese
971static void fully_qualify_uri(request_rec *r)
972{
973    if (r->method_number == M_CONNECT) {
974        return;
975    }
976    else if (!is_absolute_uri(r->filename, NULL)) {
977        const char *thisserver;
978        char *thisport;
979        int port;
980
981        thisserver = ap_get_server_name_for_url(r);
982        port = ap_get_server_port(r);
983        thisport = ap_is_default_port(port, r)
984                   ? ""
985                   : apr_psprintf(r->pool, ":%u", port);
986
987        r->filename = apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%s://%s%s%s%s", view japanese
988                                   ap_http_scheme(r), thisserver, thisport,
989                                   (*r->filename == '/') ? "" : "/",
990                                   r->filename);
991    }
992
993    return;
994}
995
996static int startsWith(request_rec *r, const char *haystack, const char *needle) {
997    int rc = (ap_strstr_c(haystack, needle) == haystack);
998    rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "prefix_stat startsWith(%s, %s) %d", haystack, needle, rc);
999    return rc;
1000}
1001/*
1002 * stat() only the first segment of a path, and only if it matches the output of the last matching rule
1003 */ view japanese
1004static int prefix_stat(request_rec *r, const char *path, apr_pool_t *pool, rewriterule_entry *lastsub)
1005{
1006    const char *curpath = path;
1007    const char *root;
1008    const char *slash;
1009    char *statpath;
1010    apr_status_t rv;
1011
1012    rv = apr_filepath_root(&root, &curpath, APR_FILEPATH_TRUENAME, pool);
1013
1014    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1015        return 0;
1016    }
1017
1018    /* let's recognize slashes only, the mod_rewrite semantics are opaque
1019     * enough.
1020     */ view japanese
1021    if ((slash = ap_strchr_c(curpath, '/')) != NULL) {
1022        rv = apr_filepath_merge(&statpath, root,
1023                                apr_pstrndup(pool, curpath,
1024                                             (apr_size_t)(slash - curpath)),
1025                                APR_FILEPATH_NOTABOVEROOT |
1026                                APR_FILEPATH_NOTRELATIVE, pool);
1027    }
1028    else {
1029        rv = apr_filepath_merge(&statpath, root, curpath,
1030                                APR_FILEPATH_NOTABOVEROOT |
1031                                APR_FILEPATH_NOTRELATIVE, pool);
1032    }
1033
1034    if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
1035        apr_finfo_t sb;
1036
1037        if (apr_stat(&sb, statpath, APR_FINFO_MIN, pool) == APR_SUCCESS) {
1038            if (!lastsub) {
1039                rewritelog(r, 3, NULL, "prefix_stat no lastsub subst prefix %s", statpath);
1040                return 1;
1041            }
1042
1043            rewritelog(r, 3, NULL, "prefix_stat compare statpath %s and lastsub output %s STATOK %d ",
1044                    statpath, lastsub->output, lastsub->flags & RULEFLAG_UNSAFE_PREFIX_STAT);
1045            if (lastsub->flags & RULEFLAG_UNSAFE_PREFIX_STAT) {
1046                return 1;
1047            }
1048            else {
1049                const char *docroot = ap_document_root(r);
1050                const char *context_docroot = ap_context_document_root(r);
1051                /*
1052                 * As an example, path (r->filename) is /var/foo/bar/baz.html
1053                 * even if the flag is not set,  we can accept a rule that
1054                 * began with a literal /var (stapath), or if the entire path
1055                 * starts with the docroot or context document root
1056                 */ view japanese
1057                if (startsWith(r, lastsub->output, statpath) ||
1058                        startsWith(r, path, docroot) ||
1059                        ((docroot != context_docroot) &&
1060                          startsWith(r, path, context_docroot))) {
1061                    return 1;
1062                }
1063            }
1064        }
1065    }
1066
1067    /* prefix will be added */ view japanese
1068    return 0;
1069}
1070
1071/*
1072 * substitute the prefix path 'match' in 'input' with 'subst' (RewriteBase)
1073 */ view japanese
1074static char *subst_prefix_path(request_rec *r, char *input, const char *match,
1075                               const char *subst)
1076{
1077    apr_size_t len = strlen(match);
1078
1079    if (len && match[len - 1] == '/') {
1080        --len;
1081    }
1082
1083    if (!strncmp(input, match, len) && input[len++] == '/') {
1084        apr_size_t slen, outlen;
1085        char *output;
1086
1087        rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "strip matching prefix: %s -> %s", input,
1088                   input+len);
1089
1090        slen = strlen(subst);
1091        if (slen && subst[slen - 1] != '/') {
1092            ++slen;
1093        }
1094
1095        outlen = strlen(input) + slen - len;
1096        output = apr_palloc(r->pool, outlen + 1); /* don't forget the \0 */
1097
1098        memcpy(output, subst, slen);
1099        if (slen && !output[slen-1]) {
1100            output[slen-1] = '/';
1101        }
1102        memcpy(output+slen, input+len, outlen - slen);
1103        output[outlen] = '\0';
1104
1105        rewritelog(r, 4, NULL, "add subst prefix: %s -> %s", input+len,
1106                   output);
1107
1108        return output;
1109    }
1110
1111    /* prefix didn't match */ view japanese
1112    return input;
1113}
1114
1115
1116/*
1117 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
1118 * |                                                       |
1119 * |                    caching support
1120 * |                                                       |
1121 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
1122 */ view japanese
1123
1124static void set_cache_value(const char *name, apr_time_t t, char *key,
1125                            char *val)
1126{
1127    cachedmap *map;
1128
1129    if (cachep) {
1130#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1131        apr_thread_mutex_lock(cachep->lock);
1132#endif
1133        map = apr_hash_get(cachep->maps, name, APR_HASH_KEY_STRING);
1134
1135        if (!map) {
1136            apr_pool_t *p;
1137
1138            if (apr_pool_create(&p, cachep->pool) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1139#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1140                apr_thread_mutex_unlock(cachep->lock);
1141#endif
1142                return;
1143            }
1144            apr_pool_tag(p, "rewrite_cachedmap");
1145
1146            map = apr_palloc(cachep->pool, sizeof(cachedmap));
1147            map->pool = p;
1148            map->entries = apr_hash_make(map->pool);
1149            map->mtime = t;
1150
1151            apr_hash_set(cachep->maps, name, APR_HASH_KEY_STRING, map);
1152        }
1153        else if (map->mtime != t) {
1154            apr_pool_clear(map->pool);
1155            map->entries = apr_hash_make(map->pool);
1156            map->mtime = t;
1157        }
1158
1159        /* Now we should have a valid map->entries hash, where we
1160         * can store our value.
1161         *
1162         * We need to copy the key and the value into OUR pool,
1163         * so that we don't leave it during the r->pool cleanup.
1164         */ view japanese
1165        apr_hash_set(map->entries,
1166                     apr_pstrdup(map->pool, key), APR_HASH_KEY_STRING,
1167                     apr_pstrdup(map->pool, val));
1168
1169#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1170        apr_thread_mutex_unlock(cachep->lock);
1171#endif
1172    }
1173
1174    return;
1175}
1176
1177static char *get_cache_value(const char *name, apr_time_t t, char *key,
1178                             apr_pool_t *p)
1179{
1180    cachedmap *map;
1181    char *val = NULL;
1182
1183    if (cachep) {
1184#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1185        apr_thread_mutex_lock(cachep->lock);
1186#endif
1187        map = apr_hash_get(cachep->maps, name, APR_HASH_KEY_STRING);
1188
1189        if (map) {
1190            /* if this map is outdated, forget it. */ view japanese
1191            if (map->mtime != t) {
1192                apr_pool_clear(map->pool);
1193                map->entries = apr_hash_make(map->pool);
1194                map->mtime = t;
1195            }
1196            else {
1197                val = apr_hash_get(map->entries, key, APR_HASH_KEY_STRING);
1198                if (val) {
1199                    /* copy the cached value into the supplied pool,
1200                     * where it belongs (r->pool usually)
1201                     */ view japanese
1202                    val = apr_pstrdup(p, val);
1203                }
1204            }
1205        }
1206
1207#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1208        apr_thread_mutex_unlock(cachep->lock);
1209#endif
1210    }
1211
1212    return val;
1213}
1214
1215static int init_cache(apr_pool_t *p)
1216{
1217    cachep = apr_palloc(p, sizeof(cache));
1218    if (apr_pool_create(&cachep->pool, p) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1219        cachep = NULL; /* turns off cache */ view japanese
1220        return 0;
1221    }
1222    apr_pool_tag(cachep->pool, "rewrite_cachep");
1223
1224    cachep->maps = apr_hash_make(cachep->pool);
1225#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1226    (void)apr_thread_mutex_create(&(cachep->lock), APR_THREAD_MUTEX_DEFAULT, p);
1227#endif
1228
1229    return 1;
1230}
1231
1232
1233/*
1234 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
1235 * |                                                       |
1236 * |                    Map Functions
1237 * |                                                       |
1238 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
1239 */ view japanese
1240
1241/*
1242 * General Note: key is already a fresh string, created (expanded) just
1243 * for the purpose to be passed in here. So one can modify key itself.
1244 */ view japanese
1245
1246static char *rewrite_mapfunc_toupper(request_rec *r, char *key)
1247{
1248    ap_str_toupper(key);
1249
1250    return key;
1251}
1252
1253static char *rewrite_mapfunc_tolower(request_rec *r, char *key)
1254{
1255    ap_str_tolower(key);
1256
1257    return key;
1258}
1259
1260static char *rewrite_mapfunc_escape(request_rec *r, char *key)
1261{
1262    return ap_escape_uri(r->pool, key);
1263}
1264
1265static char *rewrite_mapfunc_unescape(request_rec *r, char *key)
1266{
1267    ap_unescape_url(key);
1268
1269    return key;
1270}
1271
1272static char *select_random_value_part(request_rec *r, char *value)
1273{
1274    char *p = value;
1275    unsigned n = 1;
1276
1277    /* count number of distinct values */ view japanese
1278    while ((p = ap_strchr(p, '|')) != NULL) {
1279        ++n;
1280        ++p;
1281    }
1282
1283    if (n > 1) {
1284        n = ap_random_pick(1, n);
1285
1286        /* extract it from the whole string */ view japanese
1287        while (--n && (value = ap_strchr(value, '|')) != NULL) {
1288            ++value;
1289        }
1290
1291        if (value) { /* should not be NULL, but ... */ view japanese
1292            p = ap_strchr(value, '|');
1293            if (p) {
1294                *p = '\0';
1295            }
1296        }
1297    }
1298
1299    return value;
1300}
1301
1302/* child process code */ view japanese
1303static void rewrite_child_errfn(apr_pool_t *p, apr_status_t err,
1304                                const char *desc)
1305{
1306    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, err, NULL, APLOGNO(00653) "%s", desc);
1307}
1308
1309static apr_status_t rewritemap_program_child(apr_pool_t *p,
1310                                             const char *progname, char **argv,
1311                                             const char *user, const char *group,
1312                                             apr_file_t **fpout,
1313                                             apr_file_t **fpin)
1314{
1315    apr_status_t rc;
1316    apr_procattr_t *procattr;
1317    apr_proc_t *procnew;
1318
1319    if (   APR_SUCCESS == (rc=apr_procattr_create(&procattr, p))
1320        && APR_SUCCESS == (rc=apr_procattr_io_set(procattr, APR_FULL_BLOCK,
1321                                                  APR_FULL_BLOCK, APR_NO_PIPE))
1322        && APR_SUCCESS == (rc=apr_procattr_dir_set(procattr,
1323                                             ap_make_dirstr_parent(p, argv[0])))
1324        && (!user || APR_SUCCESS == (rc=apr_procattr_user_set(procattr, user, "")))
1325        && (!group || APR_SUCCESS == (rc=apr_procattr_group_set(procattr, group)))
1326        && APR_SUCCESS == (rc=apr_procattr_cmdtype_set(procattr, APR_PROGRAM))
1327        && APR_SUCCESS == (rc=apr_procattr_child_errfn_set(procattr,
1328                                                           rewrite_child_errfn))
1329        && APR_SUCCESS == (rc=apr_procattr_error_check_set(procattr, 1))) {
1330
1331        procnew = apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(*procnew));
1332        rc = apr_proc_create(procnew, argv[0], (const char **)argv, NULL,
1333                             procattr, p);
1334
1335        if (rc == APR_SUCCESS) {
1336            apr_pool_note_subprocess(p, procnew, APR_KILL_AFTER_TIMEOUT);
1337
1338            if (fpin) {
1339                (*fpin) = procnew->in;
1340            }
1341
1342            if (fpout) {
1343                (*fpout) = procnew->out;
1344            }
1345        }
1346    }
1347
1348    return (rc);
1349}
1350
1351static apr_status_t run_rewritemap_programs(server_rec *s, apr_pool_t *p)
1352{
1353    rewrite_server_conf *conf;
1354    apr_hash_index_t *hi;
1355    apr_status_t rc;
1356
1357    conf = ap_get_module_config(s->module_config, &rewrite_module);
1358
1359    /*  If the engine isn't turned on,
1360     *  don't even try to do anything.
1361     */ view japanese
1362    if (conf->state == ENGINE_DISABLED) {
1363        return APR_SUCCESS;
1364    }
1365
1366    for (hi = apr_hash_first(p, conf->rewritemaps); hi; hi = apr_hash_next(hi)){
1367        apr_file_t *fpin = NULL;
1368        apr_file_t *fpout = NULL;
1369        rewritemap_entry *map;
1370        void *val;
1371
1372        apr_hash_this(hi, NULL, NULL, &val);
1373        map = val;
1374
1375        if (map->type != MAPTYPE_PRG) {
1376            continue;
1377        }
1378        if (!(map->argv[0]) || !*(map->argv[0]) || map->fpin || map->fpout) {
1379            continue;
1380        }
1381
1382        rc = rewritemap_program_child(p, map->argv[0], map->argv,
1383                                      map->user, map->group,
1384                                      &fpout, &fpin);
1385        if (rc != APR_SUCCESS || fpin == NULL || fpout == NULL) {
1386            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rc, s, APLOGNO(00654)
1387                         "mod_rewrite: could not start RewriteMap "
1388                         "program %s", map->checkfile);
1389            return rc;
1390        }
1391        map->fpin  = fpin;
1392        map->fpout = fpout;
1393    }
1394
1395    return APR_SUCCESS;
1396}
1397
1398
1399/*
1400 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
1401 * |                                                       |
1402 * |                  Lookup functions
1403 * |                                                       |
1404 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
1405 */ view japanese
1406
1407static char *lookup_map_txtfile(request_rec *r, const char *file, char *key)
1408{
1409    apr_file_t *fp = NULL;
1410    char line[REWRITE_MAX_TXT_MAP_LINE + 1]; /* +1 for \0 */ view japanese
1411    char *value, *keylast;
1412    apr_status_t rv;
1413
1414    if ((rv = apr_file_open(&fp, file, APR_READ|APR_BUFFERED, APR_OS_DEFAULT,
1415                            r->pool)) != APR_SUCCESS)
1416    {
1417        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00655)
1418                      "mod_rewrite: can't open text RewriteMap file %s", file);
1419        return NULL;
1420    }
1421
1422    keylast = key + strlen(key);
1423    value = NULL;
1424    while (apr_file_gets(line, sizeof(line), fp) == APR_SUCCESS) {
1425        char *p, *c;
1426
1427        /* ignore comments and lines starting with whitespaces */ view japanese
1428        if (*line == '#' || apr_isspace(*line)) {
1429            continue;
1430        }
1431
1432        p = line;
1433        c = key;
1434        while (c < keylast && *p == *c && !apr_isspace(*p)) {
1435            ++p;
1436            ++c;
1437        }
1438
1439        /* key doesn't match - ignore. */ view japanese
1440        if (c != keylast || !apr_isspace(*p)) {
1441            continue;
1442        }
1443
1444        /* jump to the value */ view japanese
1445        while (apr_isspace(*p)) {
1446            ++p;
1447        }
1448
1449        /* no value? ignore */ view japanese
1450        if (!*p) {
1451            continue;
1452        }
1453
1454        /* extract the value and return. */ view japanese
1455        c = p;
1456        while (*p && !apr_isspace(*p)) {
1457            ++p;
1458        }
1459        value = apr_pstrmemdup(r->pool, c, p - c);
1460        break;
1461    }
1462    apr_file_close(fp);
1463
1464    return value;
1465}
1466
1467static char *lookup_map_dbmfile(request_rec *r, const char *file,
1468                                const char *dbmtype, char *key)
1469{
1470#if APU_MAJOR_VERSION > 1 || (APU_MAJOR_VERSION == 1 && APU_MINOR_VERSION >= 7)
1471    const apr_dbm_driver_t *driver;
1472    const apu_err_t *err;
1473#endif
1474    apr_dbm_t *dbmfp = NULL;
1475    apr_datum_t dbmkey;
1476    apr_datum_t dbmval;
1477    char *value;
1478    apr_status_t rv;
1479
1480#if APU_MAJOR_VERSION > 1 || (APU_MAJOR_VERSION == 1 && APU_MINOR_VERSION >= 7)
1481    if ((rv = apr_dbm_get_driver(&driver, dbmtype, &err,
1482            r->pool)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1483        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(10287)
1484                "mod_rewrite: can't load DBM library '%s': %s",
1485                     err->reason, err->msg);
1486        return NULL;
1487    }
1488    if ((rv = apr_dbm_open2(&dbmfp, driver, file, APR_DBM_READONLY,
1489                              APR_OS_DEFAULT, r->pool)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1490        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00656)
1491                      "mod_rewrite: can't open DBM RewriteMap %s", file);
1492        return NULL;
1493    }
1494#else
1495    if ((rv = apr_dbm_open_ex(&dbmfp, dbmtype, file, APR_DBM_READONLY,
1496                              APR_OS_DEFAULT, r->pool)) != APR_SUCCESS)
1497    {
1498        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00656)
1499                      "mod_rewrite: can't open DBM RewriteMap %s", file);
1500        return NULL;
1501    }
1502#endif
1503
1504    dbmkey.dptr  = key;
1505    dbmkey.dsize = strlen(key);
1506
1507    if (apr_dbm_fetch(dbmfp, dbmkey, &dbmval) == APR_SUCCESS && dbmval.dptr) {
1508        value = apr_pstrmemdup(r->pool, dbmval.dptr, dbmval.dsize);
1509    }
1510    else {
1511        value = NULL;
1512    }
1513
1514    apr_dbm_close(dbmfp);
1515
1516    return value;
1517}
1518static char *lookup_map_dbd(request_rec *r, char *key, const char *label)
1519{
1520    apr_status_t rv;
1521    apr_dbd_prepared_t *stmt;
1522    const char *errmsg;
1523    apr_dbd_results_t *res = NULL;
1524    apr_dbd_row_t *row = NULL;
1525    char *ret = NULL;
1526    int n = 0;
1527    ap_dbd_t *db = dbd_acquire(r);
1528    
1529    if (db == NULL) {
1530        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(02963)
1531                      "rewritemap: No db handle available! "
1532                      "Check your database access");
1533        return NULL;
1534   }
1535
1536    stmt = apr_hash_get(db->prepared, label, APR_HASH_KEY_STRING);
1537
1538    rv = apr_dbd_pvselect(db->driver, r->pool, db->handle, &res,
1539                          stmt, 0, key, NULL);
1540    if (rv != 0) {
1541        errmsg = apr_dbd_error(db->driver, db->handle, rv);
1542        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00657)
1543                      "rewritemap: error %s querying for %s", errmsg, key);
1544        return NULL;
1545    }
1546    while ((rv = apr_dbd_get_row(db->driver, r->pool, res, &row, -1)) == 0) {
1547        ++n;
1548        if (ret == NULL) {
1549            ret = apr_pstrdup(r->pool,
1550                              apr_dbd_get_entry(db->driver, row, 0));
1551        }
1552        else {
1553            /* randomise crudely amongst multiple results */ view japanese
1554            if ((double)rand() < (double)RAND_MAX/(double)n) {
1555                ret = apr_pstrdup(r->pool,
1556                                  apr_dbd_get_entry(db->driver, row, 0));
1557            }
1558        }
1559    }
1560    if (rv != -1) {
1561        errmsg = apr_dbd_error(db->driver, db->handle, rv);
1562        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00658)
1563                      "rewritemap: error %s looking up %s", errmsg, key);
1564    }
1565    switch (n) {
1566    case 0:
1567        return NULL;
1568    case 1:
1569        return ret;
1570    default:
1571        /* what's a fair rewritelog level for this? */ view japanese
1572        rewritelog(r, 3, NULL, "Multiple values found for %s", key);
1573        return ret;
1574    }
1575}
1576
1577static char *lookup_map_program(request_rec *r, apr_file_t *fpin,
1578                                apr_file_t *fpout, char *key)
1579{
1580    char *buf;
1581    char c;
1582    apr_size_t i, nbytes, combined_len = 0;
1583    apr_status_t rv;
1584    const char *eol = APR_EOL_STR;
1585    apr_size_t eolc = 0;
1586    int found_nl = 0;
1587    result_list *buflist = NULL, *curbuf = NULL;
1588
1589#ifndef NO_WRITEV
1590    struct iovec iova[2];
1591    apr_size_t niov;
1592#endif
1593
1594    /* when `RewriteEngine off' was used in the per-server
1595     * context then the rewritemap-programs were not spawned.
1596     * In this case using such a map (usually in per-dir context)
1597     * is useless because it is not available.
1598     *
1599     * newlines in the key leave bytes in the pipe and cause
1600     * bad things to happen (next map lookup will use the chars
1601     * after the \n instead of the new key etc etc - in other words,
1602     * the Rewritemap falls out of sync with the requests).
1603     */ view japanese
1604    if (fpin == NULL || fpout == NULL || ap_strchr(key, '\n')) {
1605        return NULL;
1606    }
1607
1608    /* take the lock */ view japanese
1609    if (rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire) {
1610        rv = apr_global_mutex_lock(rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire);
1611        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1612            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00659)
1613                          "apr_global_mutex_lock(rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire) "
1614                          "failed");
1615            return NULL; /* Maybe this should be fatal? */
1616        }
1617    }
1618
1619    /* write out the request key */ view japanese
1620#ifdef NO_WRITEV
1621    nbytes = strlen(key);
1622    /* XXX: error handling */
1623    apr_file_write_full(fpin, key, nbytes, NULL);
1624    nbytes = 1;
1625    apr_file_write_full(fpin, "\n", nbytes, NULL);
1626#else
1627    iova[0].iov_base = key;
1628    iova[0].iov_len = strlen(key);
1629    iova[1].iov_base = "\n";
1630    iova[1].iov_len = 1;
1631
1632    niov = 2;
1633    /* XXX: error handling */ view japanese
1634    apr_file_writev_full(fpin, iova, niov, &nbytes);
1635#endif
1636
1637    buf = apr_palloc(r->pool, REWRITE_PRG_MAP_BUF + 1);
1638
1639    /* read in the response value */ view japanese
1640    nbytes = 1;
1641    apr_file_read(fpout, &c, &nbytes);
1642    do {
1643        i = 0;
1644        while (nbytes == 1 && (i < REWRITE_PRG_MAP_BUF)) {
1645            if (c == eol[eolc]) {
1646                if (!eol[++eolc]) {
1647                    /* remove eol from the buffer */ view japanese
1648                    --eolc;
1649                    if (i < eolc) {
1650                        curbuf->len -= eolc-i;
1651                        i = 0;
1652                    }
1653                    else {
1654                        i -= eolc;
1655                    }
1656                    ++found_nl;
1657                    break;
1658                }
1659            }
1660
1661            /* only partial (invalid) eol sequence -> reset the counter */ view japanese
1662            else if (eolc) {
1663                eolc = 0;
1664            }
1665
1666            /* catch binary mode, e.g. on Win32 */ view japanese
1667            else if (c == '\n') {
1668                ++found_nl;
1669                break;
1670            }
1671
1672            buf[i++] = c;
1673            apr_file_read(fpout, &c, &nbytes);
1674        }
1675
1676        /* well, if there wasn't a newline yet, we need to read further */ view japanese
1677        if (buflist || (nbytes == 1 && !found_nl)) {
1678            if (!buflist) {
1679                curbuf = buflist = apr_palloc(r->pool, sizeof(*buflist));
1680            }
1681            else if (i) {
1682                curbuf->next = apr_palloc(r->pool, sizeof(*buflist));
1683                curbuf = curbuf->next;
1684
1685            }
1686            curbuf->next = NULL;
1687
1688            if (i) {
1689                curbuf->string = buf;
1690                curbuf->len = i;
1691                combined_len += i;
1692                buf = apr_palloc(r->pool, REWRITE_PRG_MAP_BUF);
1693            }
1694
1695            if (nbytes == 1 && !found_nl) {
1696                continue;
1697            }
1698        }
1699
1700        break;
1701    } while (1);
1702
1703    /* concat the stuff */ view japanese
1704    if (buflist) {
1705        char *p;
1706
1707        p = buf = apr_palloc(r->pool, combined_len + 1); /* \0 */ view japanese
1708        while (buflist) {
1709            if (buflist->len) {
1710                memcpy(p, buflist->string, buflist->len);
1711                p += buflist->len;
1712            }
1713            buflist = buflist->next;
1714        }
1715        *p = '\0';
1716        i = combined_len;
1717    }
1718    else {
1719        buf[i] = '\0';
1720    }
1721
1722    /* give the lock back */ view japanese
1723    if (rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire) {
1724        rv = apr_global_mutex_unlock(rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire);
1725        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1726            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00660)
1727                          "apr_global_mutex_unlock(rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire) "
1728                          "failed");
1729            return NULL; /* Maybe this should be fatal? */ view japanese
1730        }
1731    }
1732
1733    /* catch the "failed" case */ view japanese
1734    if (i == 4 && !strcasecmp(buf, "NULL")) {
1735        return NULL;
1736    }
1737
1738    return buf;
1739}
1740
1741/*
1742 * generic map lookup
1743 */ view japanese
1744static char *lookup_map(request_rec *r, char *name, char *key)
1745{
1746    rewrite_server_conf *conf;
1747    rewritemap_entry *s;
1748    char *value;
1749    apr_finfo_t st;
1750    apr_status_t rv;
1751
1752    /* get map configuration */ view japanese
1753    conf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config, &rewrite_module);
1754    s = apr_hash_get(conf->rewritemaps, name, APR_HASH_KEY_STRING);
1755
1756    /* map doesn't exist */ view japanese
1757    if (!s) {
1758        return NULL;
1759    }
1760
1761    switch (s->type) {
1762    /*
1763     * Text file map (perhaps random)
1764     */ view japanese
1765    case MAPTYPE_RND:
1766    case MAPTYPE_TXT:
1767        rv = apr_stat(&st, s->checkfile, APR_FINFO_MIN, r->pool);
1768        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1769            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00661)
1770                          "mod_rewrite: can't access text RewriteMap file %s",
1771                          s->checkfile);
1772            return NULL;
1773        }
1774
1775        value = get_cache_value(s->cachename, st.mtime, key, r->pool);
1776        if (!value) {
1777            rewritelog(r, 6, NULL,
1778                       "cache lookup FAILED, forcing new map lookup");
1779            
1780            value = lookup_map_txtfile(r, s->datafile, key);
1781            if (!value) {
1782                rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "map lookup FAILED: map=%s[txt] key=%s",
1783                           name, key);
1784                set_cache_value(s->cachename, st.mtime, key, "");
1785                return NULL;
1786            }
1787
1788            rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "map lookup OK: map=%s[txt] key=%s -> val=%s",
1789                       name, key, value);
1790            set_cache_value(s->cachename, st.mtime, key, value);
1791        }
1792        else {
1793            rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "cache lookup OK: map=%s[txt] key=%s -> val=%s",
1794                       name, key, value);
1795        }
1796
1797        if (s->type == MAPTYPE_RND && *value) {
1798            value = select_random_value_part(r, value);
1799            rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "randomly chosen the subvalue `%s'",value);
1800        }
1801
1802        return *value ? value : NULL;
1803
1804    /*
1805     * DBM file map
1806     */ view japanese
1807    case MAPTYPE_DBM:
1808        rv = apr_stat(&st, s->checkfile, APR_FINFO_MIN, r->pool);
1809        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1810            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00662)
1811                          "mod_rewrite: can't access DBM RewriteMap file %s",
1812                          s->checkfile);
1813        }
1814        else if(s->checkfile2 != NULL) {
1815            apr_finfo_t st2;
1816
1817            rv = apr_stat(&st2, s->checkfile2, APR_FINFO_MIN, r->pool);
1818            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1819                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00663)
1820                              "mod_rewrite: can't access DBM RewriteMap "
1821                              "file %s", s->checkfile2);
1822            }
1823            else if(st2.mtime > st.mtime) {
1824                st.mtime = st2.mtime;
1825            }
1826        }
1827        if(rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1828            return NULL;
1829        }
1830
1831        value = get_cache_value(s->cachename, st.mtime, key, r->pool);
1832        if (!value) {
1833            rewritelog(r, 6, NULL,
1834                       "cache lookup FAILED, forcing new map lookup");
1835
1836            value = lookup_map_dbmfile(r, s->datafile, s->dbmtype, key);
1837            if (!value) {
1838                rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "map lookup FAILED: map=%s[dbm] key=%s",
1839                           name, key);
1840                set_cache_value(s->cachename, st.mtime, key, "");
1841                return NULL;
1842            }
1843
1844            rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "map lookup OK: map=%s[dbm] key=%s -> "
1845                       "val=%s", name, key, value);
1846
1847            set_cache_value(s->cachename, st.mtime, key, value);
1848            return value;
1849        }
1850
1851        rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "cache lookup OK: map=%s[dbm] key=%s -> val=%s",
1852                   name, key, value);
1853        return *value ? value : NULL;
1854
1855    /*
1856     * SQL map without cache
1857     */ view japanese
1858    case MAPTYPE_DBD:
1859        value = lookup_map_dbd(r, key, s->dbdq);
1860        if (!value) {
1861            rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "SQL map lookup FAILED: map %s key=%s",
1862                       name, key);
1863            return NULL;
1864        }
1865
1866        rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "SQL map lookup OK: map %s key=%s, val=%s",
1867                   name, key, value);
1868
1869        return value;
1870
1871    /*
1872     * SQL map with cache
1873     */ view japanese
1874    case MAPTYPE_DBD_CACHE:
1875        value = get_cache_value(s->cachename, 0, key, r->pool);
1876        if (!value) {
1877            rewritelog(r, 6, NULL,
1878                       "cache lookup FAILED, forcing new map lookup");
1879
1880            value = lookup_map_dbd(r, key, s->dbdq);
1881            if (!value) {
1882                rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "SQL map lookup FAILED: map %s key=%s",
1883                           name, key);
1884                set_cache_value(s->cachename, 0, key, "");
1885                return NULL;
1886            }
1887
1888            rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "SQL map lookup OK: map %s key=%s, val=%s",
1889                       name, key, value);
1890
1891            set_cache_value(s->cachename, 0, key, value);
1892            return value;
1893        }
1894
1895        rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "cache lookup OK: map=%s[SQL] key=%s, val=%s",
1896                   name, key, value);
1897        return *value ? value : NULL;
1898
1899    /*
1900     * Program file map
1901     */ view japanese
1902    case MAPTYPE_PRG:
1903        value = lookup_map_program(r, s->fpin, s->fpout, key);
1904        if (!value) {
1905            rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "map lookup FAILED: map=%s key=%s", name,
1906                       key);
1907            return NULL;
1908        }
1909
1910        rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "map lookup OK: map=%s key=%s -> val=%s",
1911                   name, key, value);
1912        return value;
1913
1914    /*
1915     * Internal Map
1916     */ view japanese
1917    case MAPTYPE_INT:
1918        value = s->func(r, key);
1919        if (!value) {
1920            rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "map lookup FAILED: map=%s key=%s", name,
1921                       key);
1922            return NULL;
1923        }
1924
1925        rewritelog(r, 5, NULL, "map lookup OK: map=%s key=%s -> val=%s",
1926                   name, key, value);
1927        return value;
1928    }
1929
1930    return NULL;
1931}
1932
1933/*
1934 * lookup a HTTP header and set VARY note
1935 */ view japanese
1936static const char *lookup_header(const char *name, rewrite_ctx *ctx)
1937{
1938    const char *val = apr_table_get(ctx->r->headers_in, name);
1939
1940    /* Skip the 'Vary: Host' header combination
1941     * as indicated in rfc7231 section-7.1.4
1942     */ view japanese
1943    if (val && strcasecmp(name, "Host") != 0) {
1944        ctx->vary_this = ctx->vary_this
1945                         ? apr_pstrcat(ctx->r->pool, ctx->vary_this, ", ",
1946                                       name, NULL)
1947                         : apr_pstrdup(ctx->r->pool, name);
1948    }
1949
1950    return val;
1951}
1952
1953/*
1954 * lookahead helper function
1955 * Determine the correct URI path in perdir context
1956 */ view japanese
1957static APR_INLINE const char *la_u(rewrite_ctx *ctx)
1958{
1959    rewrite_perdir_conf *conf;
1960
1961    if (*ctx->uri == '/') {
1962        return ctx->uri;
1963    }
1964
1965    conf = ap_get_module_config(ctx->r->per_dir_config, &rewrite_module);
1966
1967    return apr_pstrcat(ctx->r->pool, conf->baseurl
1968                                     ? conf->baseurl : conf->directory,
1969                       ctx->uri, NULL);
1970}
1971
1972/*
1973 * generic variable lookup
1974 */ view japanese
1975static char *lookup_variable(char *var, rewrite_ctx *ctx)
1976{
1977    const char *result;
1978    request_rec *r = ctx->r;
1979    apr_size_t varlen = strlen(var);
1980
1981    /* fast exit */ view japanese
1982    if (varlen < 4) {
1983        return "";
1984    }
1985
1986    result = NULL;
1987
1988    /* fast tests for variable length variables (sic) first */ view japanese
1989    if (var[3] == ':') {
1990        if (var[4] && !strncasecmp(var, "ENV", 3)) {
1991            var += 4;
1992            result = apr_table_get(r->notes, var);
1993
1994            if (!result) {
1995                result = apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, var);
1996            }
1997            if (!result) {
1998                result = getenv(var);
1999            }
2000        }
2001        else if (var[4] && !strncasecmp(var, "SSL", 3)) {
2002            result = ap_ssl_var_lookup(r->pool, r->server, r->connection, r,
2003                                        var + 4);
2004        }
2005    }
2006    else if (var[4] == ':') {
2007        if (var[5]) {
2008            request_rec *rr;
2009            const char *path;
2010
2011            if (!strncasecmp(var, "HTTP", 4)) {
2012                result = lookup_header(var+5, ctx);
2013            }
2014            else if (!strncasecmp(var, "LA-U", 4)) {
2015                if (ctx->uri && subreq_ok(r)) {
2016                    path = ctx->perdir ? la_u(ctx) : ctx->uri;
2017                    rr = ap_sub_req_lookup_uri(path, r, NULL);
2018                    ctx->r = rr;
2019                    result = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, lookup_variable(var+5, ctx));
2020                    ctx->r = r;
2021                    ap_destroy_sub_req(rr);
2022
2023                    rewritelog(r, 5, ctx->perdir, "lookahead: path=%s var=%s "
2024                                "-> val=%s", path, var+5, result);
2025
2026                    return (char *)result;
2027                }
2028            }
2029            else if (!strncasecmp(var, "LA-F", 4)) {
2030                if (ctx->uri && subreq_ok(r)) {
2031                    path = ctx->uri;
2032                    if (ctx->perdir && *path == '/') {
2033                        /* sigh, the user wants a file based subrequest, but
2034                         * we can't do one, since we don't know what the file
2035                         * path is! In this case behave like LA-U.
2036                         */ view japanese
2037                        rr = ap_sub_req_lookup_uri(path, r, NULL);
2038                    }
2039                    else {
2040                        if (ctx->perdir) {
2041                            rewrite_perdir_conf *conf;
2042
2043                            conf = ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config,
2044                                                        &rewrite_module);
2045
2046                            path = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, conf->directory, path,
2047                                               NULL);
2048                        }
2049
2050                        rr = ap_sub_req_lookup_file(path, r, NULL);
2051                    }
2052
2053                    ctx->r = rr;
2054                    result = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, lookup_variable(var+5, ctx));
2055                    ctx->r = r;
2056                    ap_destroy_sub_req(rr);
2057
2058                    rewritelog(r, 5, ctx->perdir, "lookahead: path=%s var=%s "
2059                                "-> val=%s", path, var+5, result);
2060
2061                    return (char *)result;
2062                }
2063            }
2064        }
2065    }
2066
2067    /* well, do it the hard way */ view japanese
2068    else {
2069        apr_time_exp_t tm;
2070
2071        /* can't do this above, because of the getenv call */ view japanese
2072        ap_str_toupper(var);
2073
2074        switch (varlen) {
2075        case  4:
2076            if (!strcmp(var, "TIME")) {
2077                apr_time_exp_lt(&tm, apr_time_now());
2078                result = apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%04d%02d%02d%02d%02d%02d",
2079                                      tm.tm_year+1900, tm.tm_mon+1, tm.tm_mday,
2080                                      tm.tm_hour, tm.tm_min, tm.tm_sec);
2081                rewritelog(r, 1, ctx->perdir, "RESULT='%s'", result);
2082                return (char *)result;
2083            }
2084            else if (!strcmp(var, "IPV6")) {
2085                int flag = FALSE;
2086#if APR_HAVE_IPV6
2087                apr_sockaddr_t *addr = r->useragent_addr;
2088                flag = (addr->family == AF_INET6 &&
2089                        !IN6_IS_ADDR_V4MAPPED((struct in6_addr *)addr->ipaddr_ptr));
2090                rewritelog(r, 1, ctx->perdir, "IPV6='%s'", flag ? "on" : "off");
2091#else
2092                rewritelog(r, 1, ctx->perdir, "IPV6='off' (IPv6 is not enabled)");
2093#endif
2094                result = (flag ? "on" : "off");
2095            }
2096            break;
2097
2098        case  5:
2099            if (!strcmp(var, "HTTPS")) {
2100                int flag = ap_ssl_conn_is_ssl(r->connection);
2101                return apr_pstrdup(r->pool, flag ? "on" : "off");
2102            }
2103            break;
2104
2105        case  8:
2106            switch (var[6]) {
2107            case 'A':
2108                if (!strcmp(var, "TIME_DAY")) {
2109                    apr_time_exp_lt(&tm, apr_time_now());
2110                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%02d", tm.tm_mday);
2111                }
2112                break;
2113
2114            case 'E':
2115                if (!strcmp(var, "TIME_SEC")) {
2116                    apr_time_exp_lt(&tm, apr_time_now());
2117                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%02d", tm.tm_sec);
2118                }
2119                break;
2120
2121            case 'I':
2122                if (!strcmp(var, "TIME_MIN")) {
2123                    apr_time_exp_lt(&tm, apr_time_now());
2124                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%02d", tm.tm_min);
2125                }
2126                break;
2127
2128            case 'O':
2129                if (!strcmp(var, "TIME_MON")) {
2130                    apr_time_exp_lt(&tm, apr_time_now());
2131                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%02d", tm.tm_mon+1);
2132                }
2133                break;
2134            }
2135            break;
2136
2137        case  9:
2138            switch (var[7]) {
2139            case 'A':
2140                if (var[8] == 'Y' && !strcmp(var, "TIME_WDAY")) {
2141                    apr_time_exp_lt(&tm, apr_time_now());
2142                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%d", tm.tm_wday);
2143                }
2144                else if (!strcmp(var, "TIME_YEAR")) {
2145                    apr_time_exp_lt(&tm, apr_time_now());
2146                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%04d", tm.tm_year+1900);
2147                }
2148                break;
2149
2150            case 'E':
2151                if (!strcmp(var, "IS_SUBREQ")) {
2152                    result = (r->main ? "true" : "false");
2153                }
2154                break;
2155
2156            case 'F':
2157                if (!strcmp(var, "PATH_INFO")) {
2158                    result = r->path_info;
2159                }
2160                break;
2161
2162            case 'P':
2163                if (!strcmp(var, "AUTH_TYPE")) {
2164                    result = r->ap_auth_type;
2165                }
2166                break;
2167
2168            case 'S':
2169                if (!strcmp(var, "HTTP_HOST")) {
2170                    result = lookup_header("Host", ctx);
2171                }
2172                break;
2173
2174            case 'U':
2175                if (!strcmp(var, "TIME_HOUR")) {
2176                    apr_time_exp_lt(&tm, apr_time_now());
2177                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%02d", tm.tm_hour);
2178                }
2179                break;
2180            }
2181            break;
2182
2183        case 11:
2184            switch (var[8]) {
2185            case 'A':
2186                if (!strcmp(var, "SERVER_NAME")) {
2187                    result = ap_get_server_name_for_url(r);
2188                }
2189                break;
2190
2191            case 'D':
2192                if (*var == 'R' && !strcmp(var, "REMOTE_ADDR")) {
2193                    result = r->useragent_ip;
2194                }
2195                else if (!strcmp(var, "SERVER_ADDR")) {
2196                    result = r->connection->local_ip;
2197                }
2198                break;
2199
2200            case 'E':
2201                if (*var == 'H' && !strcmp(var, "HTTP_ACCEPT")) {
2202                    result = lookup_header("Accept", ctx);
2203                }
2204                else if (!strcmp(var, "THE_REQUEST")) {
2205                    result = r->the_request;
2206                }
2207                break;
2208
2209            case 'I':
2210                if (!strcmp(var, "API_VERSION")) {
2211                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%d:%d",
2212                                        MODULE_MAGIC_NUMBER_MAJOR,
2213                                        MODULE_MAGIC_NUMBER_MINOR);
2214                }
2215                break;
2216
2217            case 'K':
2218                if (!strcmp(var, "HTTP_COOKIE")) {
2219                    result = lookup_header("Cookie", ctx);
2220                }
2221                break;
2222
2223            case 'O':
2224                if (*var == 'S' && !strcmp(var, "SERVER_PORT")) {
2225                    return apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%u", ap_get_server_port(r));
2226                }
2227                else if (var[7] == 'H' && !strcmp(var, "REMOTE_HOST")) {
2228                    result = ap_get_remote_host(r->connection,r->per_dir_config,
2229                                                REMOTE_NAME, NULL);
2230                }
2231                else if (!strcmp(var, "REMOTE_PORT")) {
2232                    return apr_itoa(r->pool, r->useragent_addr->port);
2233                }
2234                break;
2235
2236            case 'S':
2237                if (*var == 'R' && !strcmp(var, "REMOTE_USER")) {
2238                    result = r->user;
2239                }
2240                else if (!strcmp(var, "SCRIPT_USER")) {
2241                    result = "<unknown>";
2242                    if (r->finfo.valid & APR_FINFO_USER) {
2243                        apr_uid_name_get((char **)&result, r->finfo.user,
2244                                         r->pool);
2245                    }
2246                }
2247                break;
2248
2249            case 'U':
2250                if (!strcmp(var, "REQUEST_URI")) {
2251                    result = r->uri;
2252                }
2253                break;
2254            }
2255            break;
2256
2257        case 12:
2258            switch (var[3]) {
2259            case 'I':
2260                if (!strcmp(var, "SCRIPT_GROUP")) {
2261                    result = "<unknown>";
2262                    if (r->finfo.valid & APR_FINFO_GROUP) {
2263                        apr_gid_name_get((char **)&result, r->finfo.group,
2264                                         r->pool);
2265                    }
2266                }
2267                break;
2268
2269            case 'O':
2270                if (!strcmp(var, "REMOTE_IDENT")) {
2271                    result = ap_get_remote_logname(r);
2272                }
2273                break;
2274
2275            case 'P':
2276                if (!strcmp(var, "HTTP_REFERER")) {
2277                    result = lookup_header("Referer", ctx);
2278                }
2279                break;
2280
2281            case 'R':
2282                if (!strcmp(var, "QUERY_STRING")) {
2283                    result = r->args;
2284                }
2285                break;
2286
2287            case 'V':
2288                if (!strcmp(var, "SERVER_ADMIN")) {
2289                    result = r->server->server_admin;
2290                }
2291                break;
