4454                ctx->vary_this = NULL;
4455                continue;
4456            }
4457            else {
4458                /* skip the rest of the chained OR conditions */ view japanese
4459                while (   i < rewriteconds->nelts
4460                       && c->flags & CONDFLAG_ORNEXT) {
4461                    c = &conds[++i];
4462                }
4463            }
4464        }
4465        else if (!rc) {
4466            return RULE_RC_NOMATCH;
4467        }
4468
4469        /* If some HTTP header was involved in the condition, remember it
4470         * for later use
4471         */ view japanese
4472        if (ctx->vary_this) {
4473            ctx->vary = ctx->vary
4474                        ? apr_pstrcat(r->pool, ctx->vary, ", ", ctx->vary_this,
4475                                      NULL)
4476                        : ctx->vary_this;
4477            ctx->vary_this = NULL;
4478        }
4479    }
4480
4481    /* expand the result */ view japanese
4482    if (!(p->flags & RULEFLAG_NOSUB)) {
4483        int unsafe_qmark = -1;
4484
4485        if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_UNSAFE_ALLOW3F) {
4486            newuri = do_expand(p->output, ctx, p, NULL);
4487        }
4488        else {
4489            newuri = do_expand(p->output, ctx, p, &unsafe_qmark);
4490        }
4491        rewritelog(r, 2, ctx->perdir, "rewrite '%s' -> '%s'", ctx->uri,
4492                   newuri);
4493
4494        if (unsafe_qmark > 0) {
4495            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(10508)
4496                    "Unsafe URL with %%3f URL rewritten without "
4497                    "UnsafeAllow3F");
4498            r->status = HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
4499            return RULE_RC_STATUS_SET;
4500        }
4501    }
4502
4503    /* expand [E=var:val] and [CO=<cookie>] */ view japanese
4504    do_expand_env(p->env, ctx);
4505    do_expand_cookie(p->cookie, ctx);
4506
4507    /* non-substitution rules ('RewriteRule <pat> -') end here. */ view japanese
4508    if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_NOSUB) {
4509        force_type_handler(p, ctx);
4510
4511        if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_STATUS) {
4512            rewritelog(r, 2, ctx->perdir, "forcing responsecode %d for %s",
4513                       p->forced_responsecode, r->filename);
4514
4515            r->status = p->forced_responsecode;
4516        }
4517
4518        return RULE_RC_NOSUB;
4519    }
4520
4521    /* Add the previously stripped per-directory location prefix, unless
4522     * (1) it's an absolute URL path and
4523     * (2) it's a full qualified URL
4524     */ view japanese
4525    if (!is_proxyreq
4526        && !is_absolute_path(newuri)
4527        && !AP_IS_SLASH(*newuri)
4528        && !is_absolute_uri(newuri, NULL)) {
4529        if (ctx->perdir) {
4530            rewritelog(r, 3, ctx->perdir, "add per-dir prefix: %s -> %s%s",
4531                       newuri, ctx->perdir, newuri);
4532
4533            newuri = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, ctx->perdir, newuri, NULL);
4534            prefix_added = 1;
4535        }
4536        else if (!(p->flags & (RULEFLAG_PROXY | RULEFLAG_FORCEREDIRECT))) {
4537            /* Not an absolute URI-path and the scheme (if any) is unknown,
4538             * and it won't be passed to fully_qualify_uri() below either,
4539             * so add an implicit '/' prefix. This avoids potentially a common
4540             * rule like "RewriteRule ^/some/path(.*) $1" that is given a path
4541             * like "/some/pathscheme:..." to produce the fully qualified URL
4542             * "scheme:..." which could be misinterpreted later.
4543             */ view japanese
4544            rewritelog(r, 3, ctx->perdir, "add root prefix: %s -> /%s",
4545                       newuri, newuri);
4546
4547            newuri = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "/", newuri, NULL);
4548            prefix_added = 1;
4549        }
4550    }
4551
4552    /* Now adjust API's knowledge about r->filename and r->args */ view japanese
4553    r->filename = newuri;
4554
4555    if (ctx->perdir && (p->flags & RULEFLAG_DISCARDPATHINFO)) {
4556        r->path_info = NULL;
4557    }
4558
4559    splitout_queryargs(r, p->flags);
4560
4561    /* If this rule is forced for proxy throughput
4562     * (`RewriteRule ... ... [P]') then emulate mod_proxy's
4563     * URL-to-filename handler to be sure mod_proxy is triggered
4564     * for this URL later in the Apache API. But make sure it is
4565     * a fully-qualified URL. (If not it is qualified with
4566     * ourself).
4567     */ view japanese
4568    if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_PROXY) {
4569        fully_qualify_uri(r);
4570
4571        rewritelog(r, 2, ctx->perdir, "forcing proxy-throughput with %s",
4572                   r->filename);
4573
4574        r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "proxy:", r->filename, NULL);
4575        return RULE_RC_MATCH;
4576    }
4577
4578    /* If this rule is explicitly forced for HTTP redirection
4579     * (`RewriteRule .. .. [R]') then force an external HTTP
4580     * redirect. But make sure it is a fully-qualified URL. (If
4581     * not it is qualified with ourself).
4582     */ view japanese
4583    if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_FORCEREDIRECT) {
4584        fully_qualify_uri(r);
4585
4586        rewritelog(r, 2, ctx->perdir, "explicitly forcing redirect with %s",
4587                   r->filename);
4588
4589        r->status = p->forced_responsecode;
4590        return RULE_RC_MATCH;
4591    }
4592
4593    /* Special Rewriting Feature: Self-Reduction
4594     * We reduce the URL by stripping a possible
4595     * http[s]://<ourhost>[:<port>] prefix, i.e. a prefix which
4596     * corresponds to ourself. This is to simplify rewrite maps
4597     * and to avoid recursion, etc. When this prefix is not a
4598     * coincidence then the user has to use [R] explicitly (see
4599     * above).
4600     */ view japanese
4601    reduce_uri(r);
4602
4603    /* If this rule is still implicitly forced for HTTP
4604     * redirection (`RewriteRule .. <scheme>://...') then
4605     * directly force an external HTTP redirect.
4606     */ view japanese
4607    if (is_absolute_uri(r->filename, NULL)) {
4608        rewritelog(r, 2, ctx->perdir, "implicitly forcing redirect (rc=%d "
4609                   "with %s", p->forced_responsecode, r->filename);
4610
4611        r->status = p->forced_responsecode;
4612        return RULE_RC_MATCH;
4613    }
4614
4615    if (!((p->flags & RULEFLAG_UNC) || prefix_added)) {
4616        /* merge leading slashes, unless they were literals in the sub */ view japanese
4617        if (!AP_IS_SLASH(p->output[0]) || !AP_IS_SLASH(p->output[1])) {
4618            while (AP_IS_SLASH(r->filename[0]) &&
4619                   AP_IS_SLASH(r->filename[1])) {
4620                r->filename++;
4621            }
4622        }
4623    }
4624
4625    /* Finally remember the forced mime-type */ view japanese
4626    force_type_handler(p, ctx);
4627
4628    /* Puuhhhhhhhh... WHAT COMPLICATED STUFF ;_)
4629     * But now we're done for this particular rule.
4630     */ view japanese
4631    return RULE_RC_MATCH;
4632}
4633
4634/*
4635 * Apply a complete rule set,
4636 * i.e. a list of rewrite rules
4637 */ view japanese
4638static int apply_rewrite_list(request_rec *r, apr_array_header_t *rewriterules,
4639                              char *perdir, rewriterule_entry **lastsub)
4640{
4641    rewriterule_entry *entries;
4642    rewriterule_entry *p;
4643    int i;
4644    int changed;
4645    rule_return_type rc;
4646    int s;
4647    rewrite_ctx *ctx;
4648    int round = 1;
4649
4650    ctx = apr_palloc(r->pool, sizeof(*ctx));
4651    ctx->perdir = perdir;
4652    ctx->r = r;
4653    *lastsub = NULL;
4654
4655    /*
4656     *  Iterate over all existing rules
4657     */ view japanese
4658    entries = (rewriterule_entry *)rewriterules->elts;
4659    changed = 0;
4660    loop:
4661    for (i = 0; i < rewriterules->nelts; i++) {
4662        p = &entries[i];
4663
4664        /*
4665         *  Ignore this rule on subrequests if we are explicitly
4666         *  asked to do so or this is a proxy-throughput or a
4667         *  forced redirect rule.
4668         */ view japanese
4669        if (r->main != NULL &&
4670            (p->flags & RULEFLAG_IGNOREONSUBREQ ||
4671             p->flags & RULEFLAG_FORCEREDIRECT    )) {
4672            continue;
4673        }
4674
4675        /*
4676         *  Apply the current rule.
4677         */ view japanese
4678        ctx->vary = NULL;
4679        rc = apply_rewrite_rule(p, ctx);
4680
4681        if (rc != RULE_RC_NOMATCH) {
4682
4683            if (!(p->flags & RULEFLAG_NOSUB)) {
4684                rewritelog(r, 2, perdir, "setting lastsub to rule with output %s", p->output);
4685                *lastsub = p;
4686            }
4687
4688            /* Catch looping rules with pathinfo growing unbounded */ view japanese
4689            if ( strlen( r->filename ) > 2*r->server->limit_req_line ) {
4690                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r,
4691                              "RewriteRule '%s' and URI '%s' "
4692                              "exceeded maximum length (%d)", 
4693                              p->pattern, r->uri, 2*r->server->limit_req_line );
4694                r->status = HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
4695                return ACTION_STATUS;
4696            }
4697
4698            /* Regardless of what we do next, we've found a match. Check to see
4699             * if any of the request header fields were involved, and add them
4700             * to the Vary field of the response.
4701             */ view japanese
4702            if (ctx->vary) {
4703                apr_table_merge(r->headers_out, "Vary", ctx->vary);
4704            }
4705
4706
4707            /* Error while evaluating rule, r->status set */ view japanese
4708            if (RULE_RC_STATUS_SET == rc) {
4709                return ACTION_STATUS_SET;
4710            }
4711
4712            /*
4713             * The rule sets the response code (implies match-only)
4714             */ view japanese
4715            if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_STATUS) {
4716                return ACTION_STATUS;
4717            }
4718
4719            /*
4720             * Indicate a change if this was not a match-only rule.
4721             */ view japanese
4722            if (rc != RULE_RC_NOSUB) {
4723                changed = ((p->flags & RULEFLAG_NOESCAPE)
4724                           ? ACTION_NOESCAPE : ACTION_NORMAL);
4725            }
4726
4727            /*
4728             *  Pass-Through Feature (`RewriteRule .. .. [PT]'):
4729             *  Because the Apache 1.x API is very limited we
4730             *  need this hack to pass the rewritten URL to other
4731             *  modules like mod_alias, mod_userdir, etc.
4732             */ view japanese
4733            if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_PASSTHROUGH) {
4734                rewritelog(r, 2, perdir, "forcing '%s' to get passed through "
4735                           "to next API URI-to-filename handler", r->filename);
4736                r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "passthrough:",
4737                                         r->filename, NULL);
4738                changed = ACTION_NORMAL;
4739                break;
4740            }
4741
4742            if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_END) {
4743                rewritelog(r, 8, perdir, "Rule has END flag, no further rewriting for this request");
4744                apr_pool_userdata_set("1", really_last_key, apr_pool_cleanup_null, r->pool);
4745                break;
4746            }
4747            /*
4748             *  Stop processing also on proxy pass-through and
4749             *  last-rule and new-round flags.
4750             */ view japanese
4751            if (p->flags & (RULEFLAG_PROXY | RULEFLAG_LASTRULE)) {
4752                break;
4753            }
4754
4755            /*
4756             *  On "new-round" flag we just start from the top of
4757             *  the rewriting ruleset again.
4758             */ view japanese
4759            if (p->flags & RULEFLAG_NEWROUND) {
4760                if (++round >= p->maxrounds) { 
4761                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(02596)
4762                                  "RewriteRule '%s' and URI '%s' exceeded "
4763                                  "maximum number of rounds (%d) via the [N] flag", 
4764                                  p->pattern, r->uri, p->maxrounds);
4765
4766                    r->status = HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
4767                    return ACTION_STATUS; 
4768                }
4769                goto loop;
4770            }
4771
4772            /*
4773             *  If we are forced to skip N next rules, do it now.
4774             */ view japanese
4775            if (p->skip > 0) {
4776                s = p->skip;
4777                while (   i < rewriterules->nelts
4778                       && s > 0) {
4779                    i++;
4780                    s--;
4781                }
4782            }
4783        }
4784        else {
4785            /*
4786             *  If current rule is chained with next rule(s),
4787             *  skip all this next rule(s)
4788             */ view japanese
4789            while (   i < rewriterules->nelts
4790                   && p->flags & RULEFLAG_CHAIN) {
4791                i++;
4792                p = &entries[i];
4793            }
4794        }
4795    }
4796    return changed;
4797}
4798
4799
4800/*
4801 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
4802 * |                                                       |
4803 * |             Module Initialization Hooks
4804 * |                                                       |
4805 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
4806 */ view japanese
4807
4808static int pre_config(apr_pool_t *pconf,
4809                      apr_pool_t *plog,
4810                      apr_pool_t *ptemp)
4811{
4812    APR_OPTIONAL_FN_TYPE(ap_register_rewrite_mapfunc) *map_pfn_register;
4813
4814    rewrite_lock_needed = 0; 
4815    ap_mutex_register(pconf, rewritemap_mutex_type, NULL, APR_LOCK_DEFAULT, 0);
4816
4817    /* register int: rewritemap handlers */ view japanese
4818    map_pfn_register = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(ap_register_rewrite_mapfunc);
4819    if (map_pfn_register) {
4820        map_pfn_register("tolower", rewrite_mapfunc_tolower);
4821        map_pfn_register("toupper", rewrite_mapfunc_toupper);
4822        map_pfn_register("escape", rewrite_mapfunc_escape);
4823        map_pfn_register("unescape", rewrite_mapfunc_unescape);
4824    }
4825    dbd_acquire = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(ap_dbd_acquire);
4826    dbd_prepare = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(ap_dbd_prepare);
4827    return OK;
4828}
4829
4830static int post_config(apr_pool_t *p,
4831                       apr_pool_t *plog,
4832                       apr_pool_t *ptemp,
4833                       server_rec *s)
4834{
4835    apr_status_t rv;
4836
4837    /* check if proxy module is available */ view japanese
4838    proxy_available = (ap_find_linked_module("mod_proxy.c") != NULL);
4839
4840    if (rewrite_lock_needed) {
4841        rv = rewritelock_create(s, p);
4842        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
4843            return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
4844        }
4845
4846        apr_pool_cleanup_register(p, (void *)s, rewritelock_remove,
4847                apr_pool_cleanup_null);
4848    }
4849
4850    /* if we are not doing the initial config, step through the servers and
4851     * open the RewriteMap prg:xxx programs,
4852     */ view japanese
4853    if (ap_state_query(AP_SQ_MAIN_STATE) == AP_SQ_MS_CREATE_CONFIG) {
4854        for (; s; s = s->next) {
4855            if (run_rewritemap_programs(s, p) != APR_SUCCESS) {
4856                return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
4857            }
4858        }
4859    }
4860
4861    return OK;
4862}
4863
4864static void init_child(apr_pool_t *p, server_rec *s)
4865{
4866    apr_status_t rv = 0; /* get a rid of gcc warning (REWRITELOG_DISABLED) */ view japanese
4867
4868    if (rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire) {
4869        rv = apr_global_mutex_child_init(&rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire,
4870                 apr_global_mutex_lockfile(rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire), p);
4871        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
4872            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, s, APLOGNO(00666)
4873                         "mod_rewrite: could not init rewrite_mapr_lock_acquire"
4874                         " in child");
4875        }
4876    }
4877
4878    /* create the lookup cache */ view japanese
4879    if (!init_cache(p)) {
4880        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, s, APLOGNO(00667)
4881                     "mod_rewrite: could not init map cache in child");
4882    }
4883}
4884
4885
4886/*
4887 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
4888 * |                                                       |
4889 * |                     runtime hooks
4890 * |                                                       |
4891 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
4892 */ view japanese
4893
4894/*
4895 * URI-to-filename hook
4896 * [deals with RewriteRules in server context]
4897 */ view japanese
4898static int hook_uri2file(request_rec *r)
4899{
4900    rewrite_perdir_conf *dconf;
4901    rewrite_server_conf *conf;
4902    const char *saved_rulestatus;
4903    const char *var;
4904    const char *thisserver;
4905    char *thisport;
4906    const char *thisurl;
4907    unsigned int port;
4908    int rulestatus;
4909    void *skipdata;
4910    const char *oargs;
4911    rewriterule_entry *lastsub = NULL;
4912
4913    /*
4914     *  retrieve the config structures
4915     */ view japanese
4916    conf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config, &rewrite_module);
4917
4918    dconf = (rewrite_perdir_conf *)ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config,
4919                                                        &rewrite_module);
4920
4921    /*
4922     *  only do something under runtime if the engine is really enabled,
4923     *  else return immediately!
4924     */ view japanese
4925    if (!dconf || dconf->state == ENGINE_DISABLED) {
4926        return DECLINED;
4927    }
4928
4929    /*
4930     *  check for the ugly API case of a virtual host section where no
4931     *  mod_rewrite directives exists. In this situation we became no chance
4932     *  by the API to setup our default per-server config so we have to
4933     *  on-the-fly assume we have the default config. But because the default
4934     *  config has a disabled rewriting engine we are lucky because can
4935     *  just stop operating now.
4936     */ view japanese
4937    if (conf->server != r->server) {
4938        return DECLINED;
4939    }
4940
4941    /* END flag was used as a RewriteRule flag on this request */
4942    apr_pool_userdata_get(&skipdata, really_last_key, r->pool);
4943    if (skipdata != NULL) {
4944        rewritelog(r, 8, NULL, "Declining, no further rewriting due to END flag");
4945        return DECLINED;
4946    }
4947
4948    /* Unless the anyuri option is set, ensure that the input to the
4949     * first rule really is a URL-path, avoiding security issues with
4950     * poorly configured rules.  See CVE-2011-3368, CVE-2011-4317. */ view japanese
4951    if ((dconf->options & OPTION_ANYURI) == 0
4952        && ((r->unparsed_uri[0] == '*' && r->unparsed_uri[1] == '\0')
4953            || !r->uri || r->uri[0] != '/')) {
4954        rewritelog(r, 8, NULL, "Declining, request-URI '%s' is not a URL-path. "
4955                    "Consult the manual entry for the RewriteOptions directive "
4956                    "for options and caveats about matching other strings.",
4957                   r->uri);
4958        return DECLINED;
4959    }
4960
4961    /*
4962     *  remember the original query string for later check, since we don't
4963     *  want to apply URL-escaping when no substitution has changed it.
4964     */ view japanese
4965    oargs = r->args;
4966
4967    /*
4968     *  add the SCRIPT_URL variable to the env. this is a bit complicated
4969     *  due to the fact that apache uses subrequests and internal redirects
4970     */ view japanese
4971
4972    if (r->main == NULL) {
4973         var = apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, REDIRECT_ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL);
4974         if (var == NULL) {
4975             apr_table_setn(r->subprocess_env, ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL, r->uri);
4976         }
4977         else {
4978             apr_table_setn(r->subprocess_env, ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL, var);
4979         }
4980    }
4981    else {
4982         var = apr_table_get(r->main->subprocess_env, ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL);
4983         apr_table_setn(r->subprocess_env, ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL, var);
4984    }
4985
4986    /*
4987     *  create the SCRIPT_URI variable for the env
4988     */ view japanese
4989
4990    /* add the canonical URI of this URL */ view japanese
4991    thisserver = ap_get_server_name_for_url(r);
4992    port = ap_get_server_port(r);
4993    if (ap_is_default_port(port, r)) {
4994        thisport = "";
4995    }
4996    else {
4997        thisport = apr_psprintf(r->pool, ":%u", port);
4998    }
4999    thisurl = apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URL);
5000
5001    /* set the variable */ view japanese
5002    var = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, ap_http_scheme(r), "://", thisserver, thisport, view japanese
5003                      thisurl, NULL);
5004    apr_table_setn(r->subprocess_env, ENVVAR_SCRIPT_URI, var);
5005
5006    if (!(saved_rulestatus = apr_table_get(r->notes,"mod_rewrite_rewritten"))) {
5007        /* if filename was not initially set,
5008         * we start with the requested URI
5009         */ view japanese
5010        if (r->filename == NULL) {
5011            r->filename = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->uri);
5012            rewritelog(r, 2, NULL, "init rewrite engine with requested uri %s",
5013                        r->filename);
5014        }
5015        else {
5016            rewritelog(r, 2, NULL, "init rewrite engine with passed filename "
5017                        "%s. Original uri = %s", r->filename, r->uri);
5018        }
5019
5020        /*
5021         *  now apply the rules ...
5022         */ view japanese
5023        rulestatus = apply_rewrite_list(r, conf->rewriterules, NULL, &lastsub);
5024        apr_table_setn(r->notes, "mod_rewrite_rewritten",
5025                       apr_psprintf(r->pool,"%d",rulestatus));
5026    }
5027    else {
5028        rewritelog(r, 2, NULL, "uri already rewritten. Status %s, Uri %s, "
5029                   "r->filename %s", saved_rulestatus, r->uri, r->filename);
5030
5031        rulestatus = atoi(saved_rulestatus);
5032    }
5033
5034    if (rulestatus) {
5035        apr_size_t flen =  r->filename ? strlen(r->filename) : 0;
5036        unsigned skip_absolute = flen ? is_absolute_uri(r->filename, NULL) : 0;
5037        int to_proxyreq = (flen > 6 && strncmp(r->filename, "proxy:", 6) == 0);
5038        int will_escape = skip_absolute && (rulestatus != ACTION_NOESCAPE);
5039
5040        if (r->args
5041                && !will_escape
5042                && *(ap_scan_vchar_obstext(r->args))) {
5043            /*
5044             * We have a raw control character or a ' ' in r->args.
5045             * Correct encoding was missed.
5046             * Correct encoding was missed and we're not going to escape
5047             * it before returning.
5048             */ view japanese
5049            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(10410)
5050                          "Rewritten query string contains control "
5051                          "characters or spaces");
5052            return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
5053        }
5054
5055        if (ACTION_STATUS == rulestatus) {
5056            int n = r->status;
5057
5058            r->status = HTTP_OK;
5059            return n;
5060        }
5061        else if (ACTION_STATUS_SET == rulestatus) {
5062            return r->status;
5063        }
5064
5065        if (to_proxyreq) {
5066            /* it should be go on as an internal proxy request */ view japanese
5067
5068            /* check if the proxy module is enabled, so
5069             * we can actually use it!
5070             */
5071            if (!proxy_available) {
5072                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00669)
5073                              "attempt to make remote request from mod_rewrite "
5074                              "without proxy enabled: %s", r->filename);
5075                return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
5076            }
5077
5078            if (rulestatus == ACTION_NOESCAPE) {
5079                apr_table_setn(r->notes, "proxy-nocanon", "1");
5080            }
5081
5082            /* make sure the QUERY_STRING and
5083             * PATH_INFO parts get incorporated
5084             */ view japanese
5085            if (r->path_info != NULL) {
5086                r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename,
5087                                          r->path_info, NULL);
5088            }
5089            if ((r->args != NULL)
5090                && ((r->proxyreq == PROXYREQ_PROXY)
5091                    || apr_table_get(r->notes, "proxy-nocanon"))) {
5092                /* see proxy_http:proxy_http_canon() */ view japanese
5093                r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename,
5094                                          "?", r->args, NULL);
5095            }
5096
5097            /* now make sure the request gets handled by the proxy handler */
5098            if (PROXYREQ_NONE == r->proxyreq) {
5099                r->proxyreq = PROXYREQ_REVERSE;
5100            }
5101            r->handler  = "proxy-server";
5102
5103            rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "go-ahead with proxy request %s [OK]",
5104                       r->filename);
5105            return OK;
5106        }
5107        else if (skip_absolute > 0) {
5108            int n;
5109
5110            /* it was finally rewritten to a remote URL */
5111
5112            if (rulestatus != ACTION_NOESCAPE) {
5113                rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "escaping %s for redirect",
5114                           r->filename);
5115                r->filename = escape_absolute_uri(r->pool, r->filename, skip_absolute);
5116            }
5117
5118            /* append the QUERY_STRING part */
5119            if (r->args) {
5120                char *escaped_args = NULL;
5121                int noescape = (rulestatus == ACTION_NOESCAPE ||
5122                                (oargs && !strcmp(r->args, oargs)));
5123
5124                r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename, "?",
5125                                          noescape
5126                                            ? r->args
5127                                            : (escaped_args =
5128                                               ap_escape_uri(r->pool, r->args)),
5129                                          NULL);
5130
5131                rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "%s %s to query string for redirect %s",
5132                           noescape ? "copying" : "escaping",
5133                           r->args ,
5134                           noescape ? "" : escaped_args);
5135            }
5136
5137            /* determine HTTP redirect response code */
5138            if (ap_is_HTTP_REDIRECT(r->status)) {
5139                n = r->status;
5140                r->status = HTTP_OK; /* make Apache kernel happy */
5141            }
5142            else {
5143                n = HTTP_MOVED_TEMPORARILY;
5144            }
5145
5146            /* now do the redirection */
5147            apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Location", r->filename);
5148            rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "redirect to %s [REDIRECT/%d]", r->filename,
5149                       n);
5150
5151            return n;
5152        }
5153        else if (flen > 12 && strncmp(r->filename, "passthrough:", 12) == 0) {
5154            /*
5155             * Hack because of underpowered API: passing the current
5156             * rewritten filename through to other URL-to-filename handlers
5157             * just as it were the requested URL. This is to enable
5158             * post-processing by mod_alias, etc.  which always act on
5159             * r->uri! The difference here is: We do not try to
5160             * add the document root
5161             */ view japanese
5162            r->uri = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->filename+12);
5163            return DECLINED;
5164        }
5165        else {
5166            /* it was finally rewritten to a local path */
5167            const char *uri_reduced = NULL;
5168
5169            /* expand "/~user" prefix */ view japanese
5170#if APR_HAS_USER
5171            r->filename = expand_tildepaths(r, r->filename);
5172#endif
5173            rewritelog(r, 2, NULL, "local path result: %s", r->filename);
5174
5175            /* the filename must be either an absolute local path or an
5176             * absolute local URL.
5177             */
5178            if (   *r->filename != '/'
5179                && !ap_os_is_path_absolute(r->pool, r->filename)) {
5180                return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
5181            }
5182
5183            /* We have r->filename as a path in a server-context rewrite without
5184             * the PT flag. The historical behavior is to treat it as a verbatim
5185             * filesystem path iff the first component of the path exists and is
5186             * readable by httpd. Otherwise, it is interpreted as DocumentRoot
5187             * relative.
5188             *
5189             * NOTICE:
5190             * We cannot leave out the prefix_stat because
5191             * - If we always prefix with document_root
5192             *   then no absolute path can could ever be used in
5193             *   a substitution. e.g. emulating an Alias.
5194             * - If we never prefix with document_root
5195             *   then the files under document_root have to
5196             *   be references directly and document_root
5197             *   gets never used and will be a dummy parameter -
5198             *   this is also bad.
5199             *   - Later addition: This part is questionable.
5200             *     If we had never prefixed, users would just 
5201             *     need %{DOCUMENT_ROOT} in substitutions or the 
5202             *     [PT] flag.
5203             *
5204             * BUT:
5205             * Under real Unix systems this is no perf problem,
5206             * because we only do stat() on the first directory
5207             * and this gets cached by the kernel for along time!
5208             */ view japanese
5209
5210            if(!(conf->options & OPTION_LEGACY_PREFIX_DOCROOT)) {
5211                uri_reduced = apr_table_get(r->notes, "mod_rewrite_uri_reduced");
5212            }
5213
5214            if (!prefix_stat(r, r->filename, r->pool,
5215                             conf->options & OPTION_UNSAFE_PREFIX_STAT ? NULL : lastsub)
5216                || uri_reduced != NULL) {
5217                int res;
5218                char *tmp = r->uri;
5219
5220                r->uri = r->filename;
5221                res = ap_core_translate(r);
5222                r->uri = tmp;
5223
5224                if (res != OK) {
5225                    rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "prefixing with document_root of %s"
5226                               " FAILED", r->filename);
5227
5228                    return res;
5229                }
5230
5231                rewritelog(r, 2, NULL, "prefixed with document_root to %s",
5232                           r->filename);
5233            }
5234
5235            rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "go-ahead with %s [OK]", r->filename);
5236            return OK;
5237        }
5238    }
5239    else {
5240        rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "pass through %s", r->filename);
5241        return DECLINED;
5242    }
5243}
5244
5245/*
5246 * Fixup hook
5247 * [RewriteRules in directory context]
5248 */ view japanese
5249static int hook_fixup(request_rec *r)
5250{
5251    rewrite_perdir_conf *dconf;
5252    char *cp;
5253    char *cp2;
5254    const char *ccp;
5255    apr_size_t l;
5256    int rulestatus;
5257    int n;
5258    char *ofilename, *oargs;
5259    int is_proxyreq;
5260    void *skipdata;
5261    rewriterule_entry *lastsub;
5262
5263    dconf = (rewrite_perdir_conf *)ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config,
5264                                                        &rewrite_module);
5265
5266    /* if there is no per-dir config we return immediately */ view japanese
5267    if (dconf == NULL) {
5268        return DECLINED;
5269    }
5270
5271    /*
5272     * only do something under runtime if the engine is really enabled,
5273     * for this directory, else return immediately!
5274     */ view japanese
5275    if (dconf->state == ENGINE_DISABLED) {
5276        return DECLINED;
5277    }
5278
5279    /* if there are no real (i.e. no RewriteRule directives!)
5280       per-dir config of us, we return also immediately */ view japanese
5281    if (dconf->directory == NULL) {
5282        return DECLINED;
5283    }
5284
5285    /*
5286     * Proxy request?
5287     */ view japanese
5288    is_proxyreq = (   r->proxyreq && r->filename
5289                   && !strncmp(r->filename, "proxy:", 6));
5290
5291    /*
5292     *  .htaccess file is called before really entering the directory, i.e.:
5293     *  URL: http://localhost/foo  and .htaccess is located in foo directory
5294     *  Ignore such attempts, allowing mod_dir to direct the client to the
5295     *  canonical URL. This can be controlled with the AllowNoSlash option.
5296     */ view japanese
5297    if (!is_proxyreq && !(dconf->options & OPTION_NOSLASH)) {
5298        l = strlen(dconf->directory) - 1;
5299        if (r->filename && strlen(r->filename) == l &&
5300            (dconf->directory)[l] == '/' &&
5301            !strncmp(r->filename, dconf->directory, l)) {
5302            return DECLINED;
5303        }
5304    }
5305
5306    /* END flag was used as a RewriteRule flag on this request */ view japanese
5307    apr_pool_userdata_get(&skipdata, really_last_key, r->pool);
5308    if (skipdata != NULL) {
5309        rewritelog(r, 8, dconf->directory, "Declining, no further rewriting due to END flag");
5310        return DECLINED;
5311    }
5312
5313    /*
5314     *  Do the Options check after engine check, so
5315     *  the user is able to explicitly turn RewriteEngine Off.
5316     */ view japanese
5317    if (!(ap_allow_options(r) & (OPT_SYM_LINKS | OPT_SYM_OWNER))) {
5318        /* FollowSymLinks is mandatory! */ view japanese
5319        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00670)
5320                     "Options FollowSymLinks and SymLinksIfOwnerMatch are both off, "
5321                     "so the RewriteRule directive is also forbidden "
5322                     "due to its similar ability to circumvent directory restrictions : "
5323                     "%s", r->filename);
5324        return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
5325    }
5326
5327    /*
5328     *  remember the current filename before rewriting for later check
5329     *  to prevent deadlooping because of internal redirects
5330     *  on final URL/filename which can be equal to the initial one.
5331     *  also, we'll restore original r->filename if we decline this
5332     *  request
5333     */ view japanese
5334    ofilename = r->filename;
5335    oargs = r->args;
5336
5337    if (r->filename == NULL) {
5338        r->filename = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->uri);
5339        rewritelog(r, 2, dconf->directory, "init rewrite engine with"
5340                   " requested uri %s", r->filename);
5341    }
5342
5343    /*
5344     *  now apply the rules ...
5345     */ view japanese
5346    rulestatus = apply_rewrite_list(r, dconf->rewriterules, dconf->directory, &lastsub);
5347    if (rulestatus) {
5348        unsigned skip_absolute = is_absolute_uri(r->filename, NULL);
5349        int to_proxyreq = 0;
5350        int will_escape = 0;
5351
5352        l = strlen(r->filename);
5353        to_proxyreq = l > 6 && strncmp(r->filename, "proxy:", 6) == 0;
5354        will_escape = skip_absolute && (rulestatus != ACTION_NOESCAPE);
5355
5356        if (r->args
5357               && !will_escape
5358               &&  *(ap_scan_vchar_obstext(r->args))) {
5359            /*
5360             * We have a raw control character or a ' ' in r->args.
5361             * Correct encoding was missed.
5362             */ view japanese
5363            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(10411)
5364                          "Rewritten query string contains control "
5365                          "characters or spaces");
5366            return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
5367        }
5368
5369        if (ACTION_STATUS == rulestatus) {
5370            int n = r->status;
5371
5372            r->status = HTTP_OK;
5373            return n;
5374        }
5375        else if (ACTION_STATUS_SET == rulestatus) {
5376            return r->status;
5377        }
5378
5379        if (to_proxyreq) {
5380            /* it should go on as an internal proxy request */ view japanese
5381
5382            /* check if the proxy module is enabled, so
5383             * we can actually use it!
5384             */ view japanese
5385            if (!proxy_available) {
5386                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(10160)
5387                              "attempt to make remote request from mod_rewrite "
5388                              "without proxy enabled: %s", r->filename);
5389                return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
5390            }
5391
5392            if (rulestatus == ACTION_NOESCAPE) {
5393                apr_table_setn(r->notes, "proxy-nocanon", "1");
5394            }
5395
5396            /* make sure the QUERY_STRING gets incorporated in the case
5397             * [NE] was specified on the Proxy rule. We are preventing
5398             * mod_proxy canon handler from incorporating r->args as well
5399             * as escaping the URL.
5400             * (r->path_info was already appended by the
5401             * rewriting engine because of the per-dir context!)
5402             */ view japanese
5403            if ((r->args != NULL) && apr_table_get(r->notes, "proxy-nocanon")) {
5404                r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename,
5405                                          "?", r->args, NULL);
5406            }
5407
5408            /* now make sure the request gets handled by the proxy handler */ view japanese
5409            if (PROXYREQ_NONE == r->proxyreq) {
5410                r->proxyreq = PROXYREQ_REVERSE;
5411            }
5412            r->handler  = "proxy-server";
5413
5414            rewritelog(r, 1, dconf->directory, "go-ahead with proxy request "
5415                       "%s [OK]", r->filename);
5416            return OK;
5417        }
5418        else if (skip_absolute > 0) {
5419            /* it was finally rewritten to a remote URL */ view japanese
5420
5421            /* because we are in a per-dir context
5422             * first try to replace the directory with its base-URL
5423             * if there is a base-URL available
5424             */ view japanese
5425            if (dconf->baseurl != NULL) {
5426                /* skip 'scheme://' */ view japanese
5427                cp = r->filename + skip_absolute;
5428
5429                if ((cp = ap_strchr(cp, '/')) != NULL && *(++cp)) {
5430                    rewritelog(r, 2, dconf->directory,
5431                               "trying to replace prefix %s with %s",
5432                               dconf->directory, dconf->baseurl);
5433
5434                    /* I think, that hack needs an explanation:
5435                     * well, here is it:
5436                     * mod_rewrite was written for unix systems, were
5437                     * absolute file-system paths start with a slash.
5438                     * URL-paths _also_ start with slashes, so they
5439                     * can be easily compared with system paths.
5440                     *
5441                     * the following assumes, that the actual url-path
5442                     * may be prefixed by the current directory path and
5443                     * tries to replace the system path with the RewriteBase
5444                     * URL.
5445                     * That assumption is true if we use a RewriteRule like
5446                     *
5447                     * RewriteRule ^foo bar [R]
5448                     *
5449                     * (see apply_rewrite_rule function)
5450                     * However on systems that don't have a / as system
5451                     * root this will never match, so we skip the / after the
5452                     * hostname and compare/substitute only the stuff after it.
5453                     *
5454                     * (note that cp was already increased to the right value)
5455                     */ view japanese
5456                    cp2 = subst_prefix_path(r, cp, (*dconf->directory == '/')
5457                                                   ? dconf->directory + 1
5458                                                   : dconf->directory,
5459                                            dconf->baseurl + 1);
5460                    if (strcmp(cp2, cp) != 0) {
5461                        *cp = '\0';
5462                        r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename,
5463                                                  cp2, NULL);
5464                    }
5465                }
5466            }
5467
5468            /* now prepare the redirect... */ view japanese
5469            if (rulestatus != ACTION_NOESCAPE) {
5470                rewritelog(r, 1, dconf->directory, "escaping %s for redirect",
5471                           r->filename);
5472                r->filename = escape_absolute_uri(r->pool, r->filename, skip_absolute);
5473            }
5474
5475            /* append the QUERY_STRING part */ view japanese
5476            if (r->args) {
5477                char *escaped_args = NULL;
5478                int noescape = (rulestatus == ACTION_NOESCAPE ||
5479                                (oargs && !strcmp(r->args, oargs)));
5480
5481                r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename, "?",
5482                                          noescape
5483                                            ? r->args
5484                                            : (escaped_args = ap_escape_uri(r->pool, r->args)),
5485                                          NULL);
5486
5487                rewritelog(r, 1, dconf->directory, "%s %s to query string for redirect %s",
5488                           noescape ? "copying" : "escaping",
5489                           r->args ,
5490                           noescape ? "" : escaped_args);
5491            }
5492
5493            /* determine HTTP redirect response code */ view japanese
5494            if (ap_is_HTTP_REDIRECT(r->status)) {
5495                n = r->status;
5496                r->status = HTTP_OK; /* make Apache kernel happy */ view japanese
5497            }
5498            else {
5499                n = HTTP_MOVED_TEMPORARILY;
5500            }
5501
5502            /* now do the redirection */ view japanese
5503            apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Location", r->filename);
5504            rewritelog(r, 1, dconf->directory, "redirect to %s [REDIRECT/%d]",
5505                       r->filename, n);
5506            return n;
5507        }
5508        else {
5509            const char *tmpfilename = NULL;
5510            /* it was finally rewritten to a local path */ view japanese
5511
5512            /* if someone used the PASSTHROUGH flag in per-dir
5513             * context we just ignore it. It is only useful
5514             * in per-server context
5515             */ view japanese
5516            if (l > 12 && strncmp(r->filename, "passthrough:", 12) == 0) {
5517                r->filename = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->filename+12);
5518            }
5519
5520            /* the filename must be either an absolute local path or an
5521             * absolute local URL.
5522             */ view japanese
5523            if (   *r->filename != '/'
5524                && !ap_os_is_path_absolute(r->pool, r->filename)) {
5525                return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
5526            }
5527
5528            /* Check for deadlooping:
5529             * At this point we KNOW that at least one rewriting
5530             * rule was applied, but when the resulting URL is
5531             * the same as the initial URL, we are not allowed to
5532             * use the following internal redirection stuff because
5533             * this would lead to a deadloop.
5534             */ view japanese
5535            if (ofilename != NULL && strcmp(r->filename, ofilename) == 0) {
5536                rewritelog(r, 1, dconf->directory, "initial URL equal rewritten"
5537                           " URL: %s [IGNORING REWRITE]", r->filename);
5538                return OK;
5539            }
5540
5541            tmpfilename = r->filename;
5542
5543            /* if there is a valid base-URL then substitute
5544             * the per-dir prefix with this base-URL if the
5545             * current filename still is inside this per-dir
5546             * context. If not then treat the result as a
5547             * plain URL
5548             */ view japanese
5549            if (dconf->baseurl != NULL) {
5550                rewritelog(r, 2, dconf->directory, "trying to replace prefix "
5551                           "%s with %s", dconf->directory, dconf->baseurl);
5552
5553                r->filename = subst_prefix_path(r, r->filename,
5554                                                dconf->directory,
5555                                                dconf->baseurl);
5556            }
5557            else {
5558                /* if no explicit base-URL exists we assume
5559                 * that the directory prefix is also a valid URL
5560                 * for this webserver and only try to remove the
5561                 * document_root if it is prefix
5562                 */ view japanese
5563                if ((ccp = ap_document_root(r)) != NULL) {
5564                    /* strip trailing slash */ view japanese
5565                    l = strlen(ccp);
5566                    if (ccp[l-1] == '/') {
5567                        --l;
5568                    }
5569                    if (!strncmp(r->filename, ccp, l) &&
5570                        r->filename[l] == '/') {
5571                        rewritelog(r, 2,dconf->directory, "strip document_root"
5572                                   " prefix: %s -> %s", r->filename,
5573                                   r->filename+l);
5574
5575                        r->filename = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->filename+l);
5576                    }
5577                }
5578            }
5579
5580            /* No base URL, or r->filename wasn't still under dconf->directory
5581             * or, r->filename wasn't still under the document root. 
5582             * If there's a context document root AND a context prefix, and 
5583             * the context document root is a prefix of r->filename, replace.
5584             * This allows a relative substitution on a path found by mod_userdir 
5585             * or mod_alias without baking in a RewriteBase.
5586             */ view japanese
5587            if (tmpfilename == r->filename && 
5588                !(dconf->options & OPTION_IGNORE_CONTEXT_INFO)) { 
5589                if ((ccp = ap_context_document_root(r)) != NULL) { 
5590                    const char *prefix = ap_context_prefix(r);
5591                    if (prefix != NULL) { 
5592                        rewritelog(r, 2, dconf->directory, "trying to replace "
5593                                    "context docroot %s with context prefix %s",
5594                                   ccp, prefix);
5595                        r->filename = subst_prefix_path(r, r->filename,
5596                                ccp, prefix);
5597                    }
5598                }
5599            }
5600
5601            apr_table_setn(r->notes, "redirect-keeps-vary", "");
5602
5603            /* now initiate the internal redirect */ view japanese
5604            rewritelog(r, 1, dconf->directory, "internal redirect with %s "
5605                       "[INTERNAL REDIRECT]", r->filename);
5606            r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "redirect:", r->filename, NULL);
5607            r->handler = REWRITE_REDIRECT_HANDLER_NAME;
5608            return OK;
5609        }
5610    }
5611    else {
5612        rewritelog(r, 1, dconf->directory, "pass through %s", r->filename);
5613        r->filename = ofilename;
5614        return DECLINED;
5615    }
5616}
5617
5618/*
5619 * MIME-type hook
5620 * [T=...,H=...] execution
5621 */ view japanese
5622static int hook_mimetype(request_rec *r)
5623{
5624    const char *t;
5625
5626    /* type */ view japanese
5627    t = apr_table_get(r->notes, REWRITE_FORCED_MIMETYPE_NOTEVAR);
5628    if (t && *t) {
5629        rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "force filename %s to have MIME-type '%s'",
5630                   r->filename, t);
5631
5632        ap_set_content_type_ex(r, t, 1);
5633    }
5634
5635    /* handler */ view japanese
5636    t = apr_table_get(r->notes, REWRITE_FORCED_HANDLER_NOTEVAR);
5637    if (t && *t) {
5638        rewritelog(r, 1, NULL, "force filename %s to have the "
5639                   "Content-handler '%s'", r->filename, t);
5640
5641        r->handler = t;
5642    }
5643
5644    return OK;
5645}
5646
5647
5648/*
5649 * "content" handler for internal redirects
5650 */ view japanese
5651static int handler_redirect(request_rec *r)
5652{
5653    if (strcmp(r->handler, REWRITE_REDIRECT_HANDLER_NAME)) {
5654        return DECLINED;
5655    }
5656
5657    /* just make sure that we are really meant! */ view japanese
5658    if (strncmp(r->filename, "redirect:", 9) != 0) {
5659        return DECLINED;
5660    }
5661
5662    /* now do the internal redirect */ view japanese
5663    ap_internal_redirect(apr_pstrcat(r->pool, r->filename+9,
5664                                     r->args ? "?" : NULL, r->args, NULL), r);
5665
5666    /* and return gracefully */ view japanese
5667    return OK;
5668}
5669
5670
5671/*
5672 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
5673 * |                                                       |
5674 * |                Module paraphernalia
5675 * |                                                       |
5676 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
5677 */ view japanese
5678
5679static const command_rec command_table[] = {
5680    AP_INIT_FLAG(    "RewriteEngine",   cmd_rewriteengine,  NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
5681                     "On or Off to enable or disable (default) the whole "
5682                     "rewriting engine"),
5683    AP_INIT_ITERATE( "RewriteOptions",  cmd_rewriteoptions,  NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
5684                     "List of option strings to set"),
5685    AP_INIT_TAKE1(   "RewriteBase",     cmd_rewritebase,     NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
5686                     "the base URL of the per-directory context"),
5687    AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("RewriteCond",     cmd_rewritecond,     NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
5688                     "an input string and a to be applied regexp-pattern"),
5689    AP_INIT_RAW_ARGS("RewriteRule",     cmd_rewriterule,     NULL, OR_FILEINFO,
5690                     "an URL-applied regexp-pattern and a substitution URL"),
5691    AP_INIT_TAKE23(   "RewriteMap",      cmd_rewritemap,      NULL, RSRC_CONF,
5692                     "a mapname and a filename and options"),
5693    { NULL }
5694};
5695
5696static void ap_register_rewrite_mapfunc(char *name, rewrite_mapfunc_t *func)
5697{
5698    apr_hash_set(mapfunc_hash, name, strlen(name), (const void *)func);
5699}
5700
5701static void register_hooks(apr_pool_t *p)
5702{
5703    static const char * const aszModProxy[] = { "mod_proxy.c", NULL };
5704
5705    /* make the hashtable before registering the function, so that
5706     * other modules are prevented from accessing uninitialized memory.
5707     */ view japanese
5708    mapfunc_hash = apr_hash_make(p);
5709    APR_REGISTER_OPTIONAL_FN(ap_register_rewrite_mapfunc);
5710
5711    ap_hook_handler(handler_redirect, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5712    ap_hook_pre_config(pre_config, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5713    ap_hook_post_config(post_config, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5714    ap_hook_child_init(init_child, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
5715    
5716    /* allow to change the uri before mod_proxy takes over it */ view japanese
5717    ap_hook_translate_name(hook_uri2file, NULL, aszModProxy, APR_HOOK_FIRST);
5718    /* fixup before mod_proxy so that a [P] URL gets fixed up there */ view japanese
5719    ap_hook_fixups(hook_fixup, NULL, aszModProxy, APR_HOOK_FIRST);
5720    ap_hook_fixups(hook_mimetype, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_LAST);
5721}
5722
5723    /* the main config structure */ view japanese
5724AP_DECLARE_MODULE(rewrite) = {
5725   STANDARD20_MODULE_STUFF,
5726   config_perdir_create,        /* create per-dir    config structures */ view japanese
5727   config_perdir_merge,         /* merge  per-dir    config structures */ view japanese
5728   config_server_create,        /* create per-server config structures */ view japanese
5729   config_server_merge,         /* merge  per-server config structures */ view japanese
5730   command_table,               /* table of config file commands       */ view japanese
5731   register_hooks               /* register hooks                      */ view japanese
5732};
5733
5734/*EOF*/

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:17-47

/*                       _                            _ _
 *   _ __ ___   ___   __| |    _ __ _____      ___ __(_) |_ ___
 *  | '_ ` _ \ / _ \ / _` |   | '__/ _ \ \ /\ / / '__| | __/ _ \
 *  | | | | | | (_) | (_| |   | | |  __/\ V  V /| |  | | ||  __/
 *  |_| |_| |_|\___/ \__,_|___|_|  \___| \_/\_/ |_|  |_|\__\___|
 *                       |_____|
 *
 *  URL書き換えモジュール
 *
 *  本モジュールは、ルールベースの書き換えエンジン（正規表現パーサーに基づく）を使用して、リクエストされたURLをオンザフライで書き換えます。
 *
 *  無制限の追加ルール条件（HTTPヘッダーを含む多くの変数で動作可能）をサポートしており、きめ細かなマッチングが可能です。また、高度なURL置換のために、外部データベース参照（プレーンテキストテーブル、DBMハッシュファイル、または外部プロセス経由）もサポートしています。
 *
 *  サーバー単位（httpd.conf）およびディレクトリ単位（.htaccess）の両方のコンテキストで、フルURL（PATH_INFO部分を含む）に対して動作し、結果としてQUERY_STRING部分を生成することも可能です。書き換え結果は、最終的に内部サブプロセス処理、外部リクエストリダイレクト、あるいは内部プロキシスループットへと導くことができます。
 *
 *  本モジュールは1996年4月に作成され、1997年7月に以下によってThe Apache Software Foundationへ独占的に寄贈されました。
 *
 *      Ralf S. Engelschall
 *      rse engelschall.com
 *      www.engelschall.com
 */

Original comment

/*                       _                            _ _
 *   _ __ ___   ___   __| |    _ __ _____      ___ __(_) |_ ___
 *  | '_ ` _ \ / _ \ / _` |   | '__/ _ \ \ /\ / / '__| | __/ _ \
 *  | | | | | | (_) | (_| |   | | |  __/\ V  V /| |  | | ||  __/
 *  |_| |_| |_|\___/ \__,_|___|_|  \___| \_/\_/ |_|  |_|\__\___|
 *                       |_____|
 *
 *  URL Rewriting Module
 *
 *  This module uses a rule-based rewriting engine (based on a
 *  regular-expression parser) to rewrite requested URLs on the fly.
 *
 *  It supports an unlimited number of additional rule conditions (which can
 *  operate on a lot of variables, even on HTTP headers) for granular
 *  matching and even external database lookups (either via plain text
 *  tables, DBM hash files or even external processes) for advanced URL
 *  substitution.
 *
 *  It operates on the full URLs (including the PATH_INFO part) both in
 *  per-server context (httpd.conf) and per-dir context (.htaccess) and even
 *  can generate QUERY_STRING parts on result.   The rewriting result finally
 *  can lead to internal subprocessing, external request redirection or even
 *  to internal proxy throughput.
 *
 *  This module was originally written in April 1996 and
 *  gifted exclusively to the The Apache Software Foundation in July 1997 by
 *
 *      Ralf S. Engelschall
 *      rse engelschall.com
 *      www.engelschall.com
 */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:75

/* XXX: これらのヘッダーは本当に必要ですか？ */

Original comment

/* XXX: Do we really need these headers? */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:115-122

/*
 * 本番環境で稼働するシステムのパフォーマンスを向上させるために、
 * コンパイラオプション -DREWRITELOG_DISABLED を使用して、
 * mod_rewrite から rewritelog コードを完全に削除することができます。
 *
 * このオプションを公開バイナリリリースに使用しないでください。
 * 使用した場合、世の中のすべての mod_rewrite に関する質問に答える責任は
 * あなた自身が負うことになります。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * in order to improve performance on running production systems, you
 * may strip all rewritelog code entirely from mod_rewrite by using the
 * -DREWRITELOG_DISABLED compiler option.
 *
 * DO NOT USE THIS OPTION FOR PUBLIC BINARY RELEASES. Otherwise YOU are
 * responsible for answering all the mod_rewrite questions out there.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:251-256

/*
 * サブリクエストが無限再帰を引き起こさないことを確認する
 *
 * サブリクエスト内ではないか、あるいはサブリクエスト内であって
 * URIがNULLではなく、かつサブ/メインのURIが異なること
 */

Original comment

/*
 * check that a subrequest won't cause infinite recursion
 *
 * either not in a subrequest, or in a subrequest
 * and URIs aren't NULL and sub/main URIs differ
 */

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:394-396

/* キャッシュされたマップにはマップ全体のmtimeが含まれており、cachep->poolのサブプールに存在します。
 * これにより、必要に応じてそれらを簡単に破棄できるようになっています。
 */

Original comment

/* cached maps contain an mtime for the whole map and live in a subpool
 * of the cachep->pool. That makes it easy to forget them if necessary.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:470-483

/* 以下に、rewritelogの有効/無効のコンパイル時選択をサポートするための、やや複雑な定義を記述する。これはC99サポートの有無に関わらず対応しており、可変引数マクロはC99でのみ利用可能である。
 *
 * do_rewritelogは、(rewritelogが有効) または (rewritelogが無効 かつ C99サポートなし) の場合に定義されるが、後者の場合は何もしない（noop）ため、最適化によって削除されるべきである。
 *
 * (rewritelogが無効) の場合、この関数はC99とそれ以外で異なる定義となる。C99の場合、rewritelog()マクロは__LINE__を渡すことで、正確な行番号のログ出力を可能にする。C99以外の場合、rewritelog()トレースに使用される行番号は常に定数となる。 */

Original comment

/* Slightly complicated definitions follow here to support
 * compile-time choice of enabled/disabled of rewritelog both with or
 * without C99 support, where varargs macros are only available with
 * C99.
 * 
 * do_rewritelog is defined for (rewritelog enabled) or (rewritelog
 * disabled AND no C99 support) but is an noop for the latter case and
 * should get optimized away.
 *
 * For the (rewritelog disabled) case the function is defined
 * differently for C99/non-C99. For C99, the rewritelog() macro passes
 * __LINE__, allowing accurate logging of line numbers. For non-C99
 * the line number used for rewritelog() tracing is always
 * constant. */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:544

/* 意図的な APLOGNO なし */

Original comment

/* Intentional no APLOGNO */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:553

/* !defined(REWRITELOG_DISABLED) || !defined(AP_HAVE_C99) */

Original comment

/* !defined(REWRITELOG_DISABLED) || !defined(AP_HAVE_C99) */

Japanese translation

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:564-578

/* スキームの文字数（'://' を含む）を返します。
 * URI が絶対パス（スキーム等を含む）である場合はその値を、
 * そうでない場合は 0 を返します。
 * supportqs が NULL でない場合、そのスキームがクエリ文字列を
 * サポートしているかどうかを返します。
 *
 * 注意: ここに新しいスキームを追加する場合は、
 *       escape_absolute_uri および splitout_queryargs を確認してください。
 *       すべてのスキームがクエリ文字列を受け入れるわけではなく、
 *       特別な処理が必要なスキームも存在します。
 *
 * XXX: 他のモジュールが実行時に mod_rewrite を拡張できるように、
 *      適切なエスケープコールバックを備えたスキームレジストリの導入を
 *      検討すべきかもしれません。
 */

Original comment

/* return number of chars of the scheme (incl. '://')
 * if the URI is absolute (includes a scheme etc.)
 * otherwise 0.
 * If supportqs is not NULL, we return a whether or not
 * the scheme supports a query string or not.
 *
 * NOTE: If you add new schemes here, please have a
 *       look at escape_absolute_uri and splitout_queryargs.
 *       Not every scheme takes query strings and some schemes
 *       may be handled in a special way.
 *
 * XXX: we may consider a scheme registry, perhaps with
 *      appropriate escape callbacks to allow other modules
 *      to extend mod_rewrite at runtime.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:795-800

/* LDAP用の特別な処理。
         * 各パーツは疑問符で区切られます。RFC 2255より:
         *     ldapurl = scheme "://" [hostport] ["/"
         *               [dn ["?" [attributes] ["?" [scope]
         *               ["?" [filter] ["?" extensions]]]]]]
         */

Original comment

/* special thing for ldap.
         * The parts are separated by question marks. From RFC 2255:
         *     ldapurl = scheme "://" [hostport] ["/"
         *               [dn ["?" [attributes] ["?" [scope]
         *               ["?" [filter] ["?" extensions]]]]]]
         */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:1051-1056

/*
                 * 例として、パス (r->filename) が /var/foo/bar/baz.html である場合、
                 * フラグが設定されていなくても、リテラルの /var (stapath) で始まるルール、
                 * またはパス全体が docroot やコンテキストドキュメントルートで始まるルールを
                 * 受け入れることができます。
                 */

Original comment

/*
                 * As an example, path (r->filename) is /var/foo/bar/baz.html
                 * even if the flag is not set,  we can accept a rule that
                 * began with a literal /var (stapath), or if the entire path
                 * starts with the docroot or context document root
                 */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:1159-1164

/* これで有効な map->entries ハッシュが作成されたはずなので、
         * そこに値を格納できる。
         *
         * r->pool のクリーンアップ時に値が失われないよう、
         * キーと値を「我々の」プールにコピーする必要がある。
         */

Original comment

/* Now we should have a valid map->entries hash, where we
         * can store our value.
         *
         * We need to copy the key and the value into OUR pool,
         * so that we don't leave it during the r->pool cleanup.
         */

Japanese translation

Original comment

/* if this map is outdated, forget it. */

                     * where it belongs (r->pool usually)

 */

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:1594-1603

/* サーバー単位のコンテキストで `RewriteEngine off` が使用された場合、
     * rewritemap プログラムは起動されませんでした。
     * この場合、そのようなマップを使用すること（通常はディレクトリ単位のコンテキスト）は、
     * 利用できないため無意味です。
     *
     * キー内の改行はパイプにバイトを残し、悪影響を及ぼします
     * （次のマップ検索が新しいキーの代わりに \n 以降の文字を使用する、など。
     * 言い換えれば、Rewritemap がリクエストと同期しなくなります）。
     */

Original comment

/* when `RewriteEngine off' was used in the per-server
     * context then the rewritemap-programs were not spawned.
     * In this case using such a map (usually in per-dir context)
     * is useless because it is not available.
     *
     * newlines in the key leave bytes in the pipe and cause
     * bad things to happen (next map lookup will use the chars
     * after the \n instead of the new key etc etc - in other words,
     * the Rewritemap falls out of sync with the requests).
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:1804-1806

/*
     * DBMファイルマップ
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:2415-2428

/* 入力文字列に対してすべての展開を実行し、
 * 結果を新しい文字列に格納する
 *
 * セキュリティ上の理由から、この展開は単一のパスで実行されなければならない。
 * そうでなければ、攻撃者が以前の展開結果に、後の展開で解釈される
 * 展開指定子を含めるように仕向けることができ、管理者が意図しない結果を
 * 生成させる可能性があるためである。
 *
 * unsafe_qmark が NULL でない場合、リテラル内またはルックアップ/展開内に
 * クエスチョンマークが見つかると、それぞれ 1 または 0 に設定される
 * （最初または最後の qmark のどちらであるかは [QSL] に依存する）。
 * -1 に初期化されるべきであり、qmark が見つからない場合はそのままである。
 */

Original comment

/* perform all the expansions on the input string
 * putting the result into a new string
 *
 * for security reasons this expansion must be performed in a
 * single pass, otherwise an attacker can arrange for the result
 * of an earlier expansion to include expansion specifiers that
 * are interpreted by a later expansion, producing results that
 * were not intended by the administrator.
 *
 * unsafe_qmark if not NULL will be set to 1 or 0 if a question mark
 * is found respectively in a literal or in a lookup/expansion (whether
 * it's the first or last qmark depends on [QSL]). Should be initialized
 * to -1 and remains so if no qmark is found.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:2640

/* この qmark は展開によるものではないため安全 */

Original comment

/* this qmark is not from an expansion thus safe */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:2643

/* 最後のqmarkに興味がある場合のみ追跡を続ける */

Original comment

/* keep tracking only if interested in the last qmark */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:2455-2457

/* 次の実際の特殊文字を見つける。そこまでの（最後の）
             * クエスチョンマークも安全である
             */

Original comment

/* find the next real special char, any (last) qmark up to
             * there is safe too
             */

Japanese translation

/* 高速パス（特殊処理なし） */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:2534-2545

/*
                 * 書き換えマップを有効にするには、ルックアップキーと
                 * デフォルト値を展開する必要があるため、再帰呼び出しを行って
                 * その処理を実行します。セキュリティ上の理由から、ネットワークから
                 * 直接取得したデータを含む文字列を決して展開してはなりません。
                 * ここでの再帰は、展開結果が lookup_map() にのみ渡され、
                 * 再展開されることはなく、再ルックアップされるだけであるため、
                 * 問題にはなりません。別の見方をすれば、この再帰は完全に
                 * ネストされた波括弧の構文によって駆動されていると言えます。
                 */

Original comment

/*
                 * To make rewrite maps useful, the lookup key and
                 * default values must be expanded, so we make
                 * recursive calls to do the work. For security
                 * reasons we must never expand a string that includes
                 * verbatim data from the network. The recursion here
                 * isn't a problem because the result of expansion is
                 * only passed to lookup_map() so it cannot be
                 * re-expanded, only re-looked-up. Another way of
                 * looking at it is that the recursion is entirely
                 * driven by the syntax of the nested curly brackets.
                 */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:2565

/* 結果としてキー変数を再利用 */

Original comment

/* reuse of key variable as result */

Japanese translation

Original comment

/* backreference */

Japanese translation

Original comment

/* ここで、サーバーまたはディレクトリごとの設定が
     * RewriteCond エントリの配列を保持している場合、それを取得し
     * 配列をクリアする
     */

Original comment

/* now, if the server or per-dir config holds an
     * array of RewriteCond entries, we take it for us
     * and clear the array
     */

Japanese translation

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4373-4375

/*
         * プロキシリクエストですか？
         */

Original comment

/*
         * Proxy request?
         */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4379-4381

/* (いわゆる) 完全なURLと照合したいため、
         * PATH_INFOのポストフィックスを再追加する必要がある
         */

Original comment

/* Since we want to match against the (so called) full URL, we have
         * to re-add the PATH_INFO postfix
         */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4388-4390

/* さらに、基盤となるファイルシステムから独立させるために、
         * URLから物理パスを取り除きます。
         */

Original comment

/* Additionally we strip the physical path from the url to match
         * it independent from the underlying filesystem.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4400-4402

/* URIをRewriteRuleパターンと照合し、
     * 適用されなかった場合は直ちに終了する。
     */

Original comment

/* Try to match the URI against the RewriteRule pattern
     * and exit immediately if it didn't apply.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4412-4414

/* 一致しました、素晴らしい！それでは、さらなる処理のために
     * コンテキスト構造を準備します
     */

Original comment

/* It matched, wow! Now it's time to prepare the context structure for
     * further processing
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4426-4431

/* OK、パターンが一致したことは既に分かっていますが、さらに
     * 全ての既存の前提条件 (RewriteCond) も真であるかを確認する必要があります。
     * 書き換えエンジンを低速化させる不要なチェックを避けるため、
     * この非常に遅い段階でこれを行います。
     */

Original comment

/* Ok, we already know the pattern has matched, but we now
     * additionally have to check for all existing preconditions
     * (RewriteCond) which have to be also true. We do this at
     * this very late stage to avoid unnecessary checks which
     * would slow down the rewriting engine.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4440

/* cond の評価中にエラーが発生しました。r->status が設定されています */

Original comment

/* Error while evaluating cond, r->status set */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4445-4447

/*
         * この条件に対してnovaryフラグが設定されている場合、vary_thisをリセットする。
         */

Original comment

/*
         * Reset vary_this if the novary flag is set for this condition.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4453

/* 一方の条件は偽ですが、もう一方は真である可能性があります。 */

Original comment

/* One condition is false, but another can be still true. */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4458

/* チェーンされたOR条件の残りをスキップする */

Original comment

/* skip the rest of the chained OR conditions */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4469-4471

/* 条件にHTTPヘッダーが含まれていた場合は、後で使用するために記憶しておく */

Original comment

/* If some HTTP header was involved in the condition, remember it
         * for later use
         */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4481

/* 結果を展開する */

Original comment

/* expand the result */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4503

/* [E=var:val] および [CO=<cookie>] を展開 */

Original comment

/* expand [E=var:val] and [CO=<cookie>] */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4507

/* 非置換ルール ('RewriteRule <pat> -') はここで終了します。 */

Original comment

/* non-substitution rules ('RewriteRule <pat> -') end here. */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4521-4524

/* 以前に取り除かれたディレクトリごとの場所プレフィックスを追加する。ただし、
     * (1) それが絶対URLパスである場合、および
     * (2) それが完全修飾URLである場合を除く
     */

Original comment

/* Add the previously stripped per-directory location prefix, unless
     * (1) it's an absolute URL path and
     * (2) it's a full qualified URL
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4537-4543

/* 絶対URIパスではなく、スキーム（もしあれば）も不明であり、
             * 下記の fully_qualify_uri() にも渡されないため、
             * 暗黙的に '/' プレフィックスを追加する。これにより、
             * "RewriteRule ^/some/path(.*) $1" のような一般的なルールが、
             * "/some/pathscheme:..." のようなパスを与えられた際に、
             * 後で誤解される可能性のある完全修飾URL "scheme:..." を
             * 生成してしまうことを回避する。
             */

Original comment

/* Not an absolute URI-path and the scheme (if any) is unknown,
             * and it won't be passed to fully_qualify_uri() below either,
             * so add an implicit '/' prefix. This avoids potentially a common
             * rule like "RewriteRule ^/some/path(.*) $1" that is given a path
             * like "/some/pathscheme:..." to produce the fully qualified URL
             * "scheme:..." which could be misinterpreted later.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4552

/* r->filename と r->args に関する API の知識を調整する */

Original comment

/* Now adjust API's knowledge about r->filename and r->args */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4561-4567

/* このルールがプロキシのスループットのために強制されている場合
     * (`RewriteRule ... ... [P]')、後のApache APIでこのURLに対してmod_proxyが確実にトリガーされるよう、mod_proxyのURLからファイル名へのハンドラをエミュレートします。ただし、完全修飾URLであることを確認してください。（そうでない場合は、自身で修飾します）。
     */

Original comment

/* If this rule is forced for proxy throughput
     * (`RewriteRule ... ... [P]') then emulate mod_proxy's
     * URL-to-filename handler to be sure mod_proxy is triggered
     * for this URL later in the Apache API. But make sure it is
     * a fully-qualified URL. (If not it is qualified with
     * ourself).
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4578-4582

/* このルールがHTTPリダイレクトのために明示的に強制されている場合
     * (`RewriteRule .. .. [R]')、外部HTTPリダイレクトを強制する。
     * ただし、完全修飾URLであることを確認すること。（そうでない場合は、
     * 自ホストの情報で修飾する）。
     */

Original comment

/* If this rule is explicitly forced for HTTP redirection
     * (`RewriteRule .. .. [R]') then force an external HTTP
     * redirect. But make sure it is a fully-qualified URL. (If
     * not it is qualified with ourself).
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4593-4600

/* 特殊な書き換え機能: 自己リダクション
     * URLから http[s]://<ourhost>[:<port>] というプレフィックス（自ホストに対応するプレフィックス）を取り除くことで、URLを短縮します。これは、書き換えマップを簡素化し、再帰などを回避するためです。このプレフィックスが偶然の一致ではない場合、ユーザーは明示的に [R] を使用する必要があります（上記参照）。
     */

Original comment

/* Special Rewriting Feature: Self-Reduction
     * We reduce the URL by stripping a possible
     * http[s]://<ourhost>[:<port>] prefix, i.e. a prefix which
     * corresponds to ourself. This is to simplify rewrite maps
     * and to avoid recursion, etc. When this prefix is not a
     * coincidence then the user has to use [R] explicitly (see
     * above).
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4603-4606

/* このルールがHTTPリダイレクト（`RewriteRule .. <scheme>://...'）に対して
     * 暗黙的に強制されている場合は、外部HTTPリダイレクトを直接強制する。
     */

Original comment

/* If this rule is still implicitly forced for HTTP
     * redirection (`RewriteRule .. <scheme>://...') then
     * directly force an external HTTP redirect.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4616

/* 先頭のスラッシュをマージする。ただし、sub 内でリテラルとして扱われていた場合は除く */

Original comment

/* merge leading slashes, unless they were literals in the sub */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4625

/* 最後に、強制されたmime-typeを記憶する */

Original comment

/* Finally remember the forced mime-type */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4628-4630

/* ふぅ……なんて複雑なものなんだ ;_)
     * しかし、この特定のルールについてはこれで完了だ。
     */

Original comment

/* Puuhhhhhhhh... WHAT COMPLICATED STUFF ;_)
     * But now we're done for this particular rule.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4634-4637

/*
 * 完全なルールセットを適用する。
 * すなわち、書き換えルールのリストを適用する。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Apply a complete rule set,
 * i.e. a list of rewrite rules
 */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4655-4657

/*
     *  既存のすべてのルールを反復処理する
     */

Original comment

/*
     *  Iterate over all existing rules
     */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4664-4668

/*
         *  サブリクエストにおいて、明示的に要求されている場合、
         *  またはこれが proxy-throughput や強制リダイレクトのルールである場合は、
         *  このルールを無視する。
         */

Original comment

/*
         *  Ignore this rule on subrequests if we are explicitly
         *  asked to do so or this is a proxy-throughput or a
         *  forced redirect rule.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4675-4677

/*
         *  現在のルールを適用する。
         */

Original comment

/*
         *  Apply the current rule.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4688

/* pathinfoが際限なく増大するループ規則をキャッチする */

Original comment

/* Catch looping rules with pathinfo growing unbounded */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4698-4701

/* 次に何を行うかに関わらず、一致するものが見つかりました。リクエストヘッダーフィールドのいずれかが関与しているかを確認し、それらをレスポンスの Vary フィールドに追加します。
             */

Original comment

/* Regardless of what we do next, we've found a match. Check to see
             * if any of the request header fields were involved, and add them
             * to the Vary field of the response.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4707

/* ルールの評価中にエラーが発生しました。r->status を設定します */

Original comment

/* Error while evaluating rule, r->status set */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4712-4714

/*
             * このルールはレスポンスコードを設定します（マッチのみを意味します）
             */

Original comment

/*
             * The rule sets the response code (implies match-only)
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4719-4721

/*
             * これがマッチのみのルールでなかった場合、変更を示す。
             */

Original comment

/*
             * Indicate a change if this was not a match-only rule.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4727-4732

/*
             *  パススルー機能 (`RewriteRule .. .. [PT]'):
             *  Apache 1.x API は非常に制限されているため、
             *  書き換えられた URL を mod_alias、mod_userdir などの
             *  他のモジュールに渡すにはこのハックが必要です。
             */

Original comment

/*
             *  Pass-Through Feature (`RewriteRule .. .. [PT]'):
             *  Because the Apache 1.x API is very limited we
             *  need this hack to pass the rewritten URL to other
             *  modules like mod_alias, mod_userdir, etc.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4747-4750

/*
             *  プロキシのパススルー、last-rule、およびnew-roundフラグの場合も処理を停止する。
             */

Original comment

/*
             *  Stop processing also on proxy pass-through and
             *  last-rule and new-round flags.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4755-4758

/*
             *  "new-round" フラグが立っている場合は、書き換えルールセットの
             *  先頭から再度開始します。
             */

Original comment

/*
             *  On "new-round" flag we just start from the top of
             *  the rewriting ruleset again.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4772-4774

/*
             *  もし次のN個のルールをスキップせざるを得ない場合は、今すぐ実行する。
             */

Original comment

/*
             *  If we are forced to skip N next rules, do it now.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4785-4788

/*
             *  現在のルールが次のルールと連結されている場合は、
             *  それらの次のルールをすべてスキップする
             */

Original comment

/*
             *  If current rule is chained with next rule(s),
             *  skip all this next rule(s)
             */

Japanese translation

modules/mappers/mod_rewrite.c:4800-4806

/*
 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
 * |                                                       |
 * |             モジュール初期化フック
 * |                                                       |
 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
 */

Original comment

/*
 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
 * |                                                       |
 * |             Module Initialization Hooks
 * |                                                       |
 * +-------------------------------------------------------+
 */

Japanese translation
